With 45 employees, it has been offering a range of infrastructure and application solutions since 2016, delivered by local teams and supported, when necessary, by the group's skills and resources.

Senior IT Data Analyst (M/F)

This is your mission

Are you passionate about data analysis, data modelling, and modern data platforms? Then this position is for you.

As a Senior IT Data Analyst, you will support a strategic multi-year transformation program focused on the re-engineering of the Life Data Warehouse and the gradual implementation of a Data Mesh architecture. You will work closely with project teams to analyze business data requirements, design robust data models, and contribute to enterprise data governance initiatives.

Build Side

  • Analyze business requirements and translate them into structured data and functional models.
  • Design and maintain conceptual, logical, and physical data models for Data Warehouse and Data Mesh initiatives.
  • Collaborate with business and technical stakeholders to define data structures, governance rules, and integration requirements.
  • Contribute to enterprise data management practices following DMBOK principles.
  • Support the implementation of modern Azure-based data platforms using Databricks, Informatica, and SQL.
  • Participate in the continuous evolution of data architecture and enterprise modelling standards.

Run Side

  • Ensure the consistency, quality, and governance of enterprise data models.
  • Perform data analysis, querying, and validation to support business and project needs.
  • Maintain and improve documentation related to data models, governance, and project deliverables.
  • Support project teams throughout the software development lifecycle by providing data analysis expertise.
  • Collaborate with development teams to ensure data solutions align with business and technical requirements.
  • Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives related to data quality, governance, and modelling practices.

This is your background

You have at least 5 years of experience in Data Analysis, Data Modelling, or Data Management within complex enterprise environments.

You enjoy translating business requirements into robust data solutions and working on strategic data transformation initiatives using modern cloud technologies.

This Is Your Expertise

  • Business & Functional Analysis
  • Data Analysis
  • Data Modelling
  • Data Management & Governance
  • DMBOK
  • SQL
  • Microsoft Azure Data Platform
  • Databricks
  • Informatica (or equivalent ETL tools)
  • Microsoft BI Tools
  • Data Warehouse concepts
  • Data Mesh principles
  • Development Lifecycle (SDLC)
  • Enterprise Data Architecture (preferred)
  • Erwin or equivalent Data Modelling tools (preferred)
  • Insurance processes (Life) (preferred)

Languages

English: Professional working proficiency (mandatory)

Dutch and/or French: Professional working proficiency (preferred depending on the project environment)

This is how you work as part of a team:

  • Independent while being a strong team player
  • Structured and proactive
  • Pragmatic and hands-on
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Excellent communication and presentation skills
  • Quality-driven and detail-oriented
  • Flexible and stress-resistant
  • Curious with a willingness to learn and share knowledge
  • Able to communicate with both technical and business stakeholders
  • Creative and dynamic

This is our commitment

At Consort Group, you are an expert who we support so that every assignment becomes a step that counts.

  • Attentive and human onboarding
  • A truly hands-on management style
  • Continuous training opportunities
  • Concrete commitments: inclusion, equality, solidarity
  • A comprehensive HR package: health insurance, meal vouchers, pension plan, and additional benefits
  • A culture of feedback and meaningful projects

It's clear

The Recruitment Process

  • An initial phone call with our recruitment team
  • An HR interview and a job interview with a business engineer
  • A technical interview with one of our experts
  • A final meeting with your future manager
  • And if we're a good fit, we'll get started together.

Good to know

Location: Brussels

Contract: Permanent contract/ Freelance

Remote work: Hybrid (2 days per week onsite)

Job category: Data / Data Analysis / Data Management

What you will do here, you won't do anywhere else.

This moment is yours.

Title: Data Science Officer - Private Debt
Description:
Data Science Officer– Private Debt | AI, Data & Transformation Focus




Kartesia is an independent and privately-owned European specialist financing small and middle-market companies. We provide long-term liquidity and credit solutions following a fundamental and disciplined approach, based on our knowledge of the European small and mid-cap corporate market and our extensive professional network.




We are looking for a forward‑thinking Data Scientist to play a key role in the digital transformation of our Private Debt platform.




You will leverage advanced analytics, artificial intelligence, and optimized data flows to enhance investment decisions, strengthen reporting capabilities, and improve operational efficiency across the full credit lifecycle and broader firm processes.




This is not a traditional data scientist position, but a transformation role focused on embedding AI into investment processes, building best‑in‑class reporting, optimizing data architecture and flows, implementing agentic solutions, and driving measurable efficiency and scalability gains.

The role sits at the intersection of data science, credit expertise, and IT transformation, with high visibility across investment, risk, and management teams.




Key Responsibilities

AI & Advanced Analytics

Design and deploy AI/ML models and LLM-based systems to enhance
Credit risk assessment
Investment performance forecasting
Scenario analysis and stress testing
Build and orchestrate agentic AI to support end-to-end investment processes
Leverage NLP and generative AI to extract insights from
Financial statements
Credit memos
Legal documentation (loan agreements, covenants)
Explore innovative use cases of AI pilots to augment investment decision‑making and insight generation




Reporting & Data visualization

Enhance end‑to‑end reporting frameworks for:
Develop interactive dashboards (Power BI)
Ensure reporting is scalable, automated, and audit‑ready
Deploy reasoning agents for AI-driven enrichment




Data Flow Optimization & Architecture

Lead the optimization of data pipelines and data flows across systems
Improve data quality, governance, and consistency across platforms
Reduce manual processes through automation and smart data structuring




IT Transformation & Process Improvement

Act as a key contributor to the data & IT transformation roadmap
Partner with investment, risk, and legal teams to design workflows around AI capabilities
Drive the adoption of agentic AI solutions and LLM platforms




Required Experience

2 to 4 years of experience in data science, analytics, or quantitative roles, ideally within private debt, asset management, banking, or consulting
Proven exposure to data transformation / IT projects as to reporting automation and redesign







Required Technical skills

Strong understanding of LLM (Claude, GPT…), alongside solid foundations in machine learning techniques, data architecture and data modeling
Proven experience designing and deploying agentic AI systems including multi-step reasoning workflows, tool integration, orchestration and human-in-the-loop approaches
Proficiency in Python and SQL for analysis, modelling
Experience with BI and data visualization tools (Power BI), data pipelines and ETL/ETL tools (Data Factory), Cloud environments (Azure, Snowflake)

Title: CRO Specialist
Description:
Wat je gaat doen
Zie jij overal kansen om websites te verbeteren en kun je dat nog eens met data staven ook? Perfect! Als CRO Specialist bij iO maak je met jouw datagedreven optimalisaties écht impact op de conversies van websites en portalen. Jij vormt de brug tussen onze marketeers en developers en geeft hen de juiste input om mee aan de slag te gaan.

Op basis van jouw aanbevelingen uit verschillende onderzoeksmethodes, zet je proactief initiatieven op die de gebruikservaring en conversies een boost geven.

Jouw dagelijkse uitdagingen:

Van snelle audits tot diepgaande analyses: Je duikt in de data voor een brede waaier aan klanten. Je voert CRO-analyses en audits uit, brengt de pijnpunten in de customer journey bloot en vertaalt je inzichten naar concrete, impactvolle aanbevelingen.
Diepgaande optimalisatietrajecten: Je bijt je vast in complexe optimalisatieprogramma's. Hierin focus je toegewijd op het continu verbeteren van de gebruikerservaring en het maximaliseren van conversies


Dit alles doe je natuurlijk niet alleen. Je werkt nauw samen met een team van data- en analytics specialisten, UX-designers, marketeers en strategen. Je voert A/B-tests uit, analyseert de resultaten en verwerkt je bevindingen in professionele presentaties. Jouw inzichten giet je in overtuigende verhalen waarmee je zowel klanten als collega’s inspireert en aanzet tot actie.

Bij iO staan we nooit stil. We investeren volop in jouw technische kennis en persoonlijke ontwikkeling. Want wij weten als geen ander: samen staan we sterker.

Daarom ben jij de perfecte CRO Specialist

Je hebt een sterk analytisch vermogen, bent van nature nieuwsgierig, werkt nauwkeurig en hebt een proactieve houding
Online personalisatietools zoals VWO, Spotler, Adobe of Blueconic hebben voor jou geen geheimen
Je bent een krak in het toepassen van CRO-principes (zoals Cialdini en andere overtuigingstechnieken) op diverse businessmodellen (B2B, B2C, e-commerce)
Met minstens 2 jaar ervaring op de teller weet jij perfect hoe je een succesvol optimalisatieplan opstelt en uitvoert
Je vindt moeiteloos je weg in tools als Clarity en Hotjar
Complexe data uit Google Looker Studio, Google Analytics en PowerBI zet jij om in overtuigende en heldere inzichten
Je hebt ervaring met HTML en CSS. Kennis van JavaScript is een dikke plus!
Je weet hoe je duidelijke en overtuigende presentaties maakt en kunt je bevindingen uitstekend onderbouwen


Dit krijg jij van ons

32 vakantiedagen
Op basis van een 40-urige werkweek heb je 20 vakantiedagen en 12 ADV-dagen.

Mobiliteit & eindejaarspremie
Na 6 maanden in dienst kun je bij iO rekenen op een eindejaarspremie. En op mobiliteitsvlak bieden we verschillende mogelijkheden, passend bij jouw job of voorkeur.

Hospitalisatieverzekering
Heb je eens iets voor? Dan is extra stress rond kosten het laatste wat je kunt gebruiken. Iedereen geniet bij ons van een hospitalisatieverzekering bij Axa.

Hybride werken
Een goede werk-privébalans is essentieel. Samen met je team maak je afspraken over wanneer én waar je werkt.

Gezonde snacks & lunch
Op elke iO campus pak je de hele dag een gezond tussendoortje en lunch je voor een kleine vergoeding gezond én gevarieerd.

Persoonlijk ontwikkelbudget
Je krijgt elk jaar € 3.000,- om jezelf te ontwikkelen, in je vak of als persoon, met trainingen, events of cursussen.

Samen vieren
Als we iets te vieren hebben, dan doen we het goed! Het partyteam op jouw campus staat op z'n tijd dan ook garant voor de leukste (thema)feestjes.
Show more

Title: Digital Analytics and CRO Consultant
Description:
Why Keyrus, Why Now!

Keyrus is an international group of 2,800 consultants and experts across 28 countries, built on a single conviction: AI does not transform businesses. Architected intelligence does. For more than 30 years, we have been building the data foundations that make intelligent systems work — designing the Operating System of the intelligent enterprise, where intelligence is embedded into the core of business processes to create sustainable value: we operationalise intelligence.

AI does not replace humans. It repositions us to a place no system can follow: understanding, deciding, designing, and creating. At Keyrus, you will not just develop skills - you will develop judgment. Your expertise sharpens with every system you architect, every client challenge you solve, every deployment that compounds on the last.

Over time, you grow into one of the rarest professionals of the intelligence era: someone who bridges data, AI, and human decision-making at scale, across industries and geographies. This is not a role you fill. It is a discipline you master and a story you help write to become a Keyrus Architect of Intelligence.

Technology amplifies. Keyrus culture differentiates. Industrial discipline connects the two.

What You'll Architect

This role focuses on turning customer behavior into measurable, continuously improving performance systems. You design the feedback loops between data, experimentation, and experience.


Design measurement frameworks across websites and apps to capture reliable user behavior data
Implement and manage tagging strategies (e.g. Google Tag Manager, Adobe Launch, Tealium)
Analyse customer journeys by combining data across analytics, transactional, and behavioral sources
Translate data insights into hypotheses that improve user experience and marketing performance
Design, run, and evaluate experimentation programs (A/B tests, CRO initiatives)
Build dashboards and visualisations that support decision-making across teams
Leverage platforms such as Google Analytics 4, Adobe Analytics, or Contentsquare to drive insights
Query and structure data using SQL across environments such as BigQuery, Azure or Databricks
Collaborate with stakeholders to ensure accurate measurement and alignment on performance goals
Contribute to building scalable customer intelligence systems combining analytics, UX, and experimentation


Who You Are


You understand that analytics is not about reporting — it’s about improving decisions and experiences
You think in loops: data → insight → hypothesis → test → improvement
You combine analytical depth with curiosity about user behavior and digital experience
You are comfortable working across tracking, data analysis, and experimentation
You bring structure to complex customer journeys and fragmented data sources


What You Bring

Qualifications / Experience


3+ years of experience in Digital Analytics, CRO, or Digital Marketing Analytics
Experience analysing digital performance across web and/or app environments
Professional working proficiency in Dutch or French and English


Technical & Functional Skills


Experience with tagging and data collection (Google Tag Manager, Adobe Launch, Tealium)
Strong knowledge of digital analytics tools (Google Analytics 4, Adobe Analytics, Contentsquare)
Experience with SQL and data querying across platforms (BigQuery, Azure, Databricks)
Ability to combine multiple data sources into coherent analysis models
Understanding of UX principles, SEO, and digital marketing performance
Experience with A/B testing, experimentation design, and CRO methodologies
Experience with data visualisation and dashboarding for business users


Nice-to-haves


Exposure to machine learning or advanced analytics in marketing


Rewards - What We Offer At Keyrus

Growth

At Keyrus we have created an environment where people are supported, challenged and connected to the people around them.

Working on impactful Data & Digital projects you will develop a broad and versatile skill set and stay ahead in a fast-evolving, AI-driven landscape.

Our digital learning platform, with over 30,000 courses including in-house trainings developed by our experts, is a perfect way to stay on top of your game

A generous training budget is available to help foster a culture where learning, feedback and continuous improvement is the norm

You will have a dedicated coach, benefit from regular check-ins, learn via different communities and have clear alignment with your team and company goals

We want you to grow with confidence. We therefore place a strong emphasis on ownership, open feedback and continuous development.

When people feel trusted, connected and inspired, they do their best work!

Rewards

Mobility budget? Car with fuel or charging car? We offer both so you can choose!

On top of that we also have daily and monthly net allowances, eco vouchers, hospitalisation (DKV) insurance and group insurance, as well as a generous bonus scheme that you can use on our flexible reward plan.

What We Stand For

Collective Intelligence — Collaboration across expertise, functions, and geographies makes it possible to combine know-how and deliver more comprehensive responses to client challenges.

Reliability — The ability to deliver complex projects with rigour is one of the pillars of our client relationship.

Pragmatism — Prioritising concrete impact and measurable value over abstract technological discourse.

Entrepreneurial Spirit — Curiosity, energy, and freedom are the foundations of our culture; they enable initiative and sustained innovation.

About Keyrus

At Keyrus, we help organizations move from experimental AI to industrialized AI, from isolated agents to orchestrated systems, and from insight to execution. This is the discipline we call being an Architect of Intelligence. Designing the Operating System of the intelligent enterprise, where intelligence is embedded into the core of business processes to create sustainable value: we operationalize intelligence.

Powered by our proprietary Human Orchestrated Model™ (HOM), we architect reliable:


Intelligence Foundations,
Human in Command governance,
and Performance Steering,


To create intelligent environments where technology amplifies human capabilities and performance compounds over time.

With 30+ years of expertise and 2,800 employees across 28 countries, we help organizations go beyond transformation: to build adaptive, resilient, and continuously improving intelligent organizations.

AI does not transform businesses. Architected intelligence does.

For over 30 years, Keyrus have been Architects of intelligent systems. We have combined engineering, business consulting, data architecture, and AI in real operational environments. We have done this whilst never forgetting a more human enterprise of allowing humans to design, supervise, and shape what only they can create.. Find out more on our services on keyrus.com.

Title: Market & Business Intelligence Expert
Description:
Wil jij met data écht impact maken op strategische beslissingen?

Als Market & Business Intelligence Expert bij Securex vertaal je complexe markt-, klant- en bedrijfsdata naar heldere inzichten en concrete acties. Zo help je mee bepalen waar we groeien, hoe we ons onderscheiden en welke keuzes het meeste verschil maken.

De functie

Als Market & Business Intelligence Expert Ben Je Een Cruciale Schakel Tussen Data En Strategie. Jij Zorgt Ervoor Dat Beslissingen Binnen Securex Onderbouwd, Relevant En Toekomstgericht Zijn. Je Verantwoordelijkheden Zijn Veelzijdig En Impactvol

Markt-, klant- en concurrentieanalyse


Je volgt trends in de markt, het gedrag van klanten en prospects en relevante macro-economische evoluties op
Je voert kwalitatieve en kwantitatieve analyses, bevragingen en benchmarkstudies uit
Je detecteert opportuniteiten en risico’s op basis van data en onderzoek


Business Intelligence & data-analyse


Je analyseert interne data en vertaalt die naar bruikbare inzichten
Je ontwikkelt KPI’s en werkt samen met data teams om dashboards en rapporteringen op te zetten
Je brengt complexe data terug tot heldere, actiegerichte inzichten


Strategische ondersteuning

Je fungeert als neutrale sparringpartner bij strategische keuzes

Uitbouw & onderhoud van een AI-ondersteund kennisplatform dat alle rapporten, studies en analyses verbindt tot bevraagbare, actiegerichte inzichten voor interne stakeholders.


Je rapporteert aan de Director Intelligence en speelt een sleutelrol in het verbinden van data, business en strategie.

Jouw profiel

Je bent een analytische denker met een sterke business mindset en het talent om data te vertalen naar heldere verhalen.

Achtergrond & ervaring


Masterdiploma (economie, statistiek, psychologie, bedrijfskunde, …) of gelijkwaardig door ervaring
3 tot 5 jaar ervaring in market intelligence, business intelligence of consultancy
Ervaring in een complexe organisatie met meerdere stakeholders is een plus


Analytische & Technische Skills


Sterke kennis van marktonderzoeksmethodes en strategische analysetechnieken (SWOT, PESTEL, Porter, …)
Ervaring met dataverwerking en analyse (Excel, SQL; kennis van SPSS, Python of R is een plus)
Je weet inzichten visueel en begrijpelijk te maken
Praktische kennis van AI om bovenstaande vaardigheden naar een hoger niveau te tillen.


Persoonlijke competenties


Sterk conceptueel en analytisch denkvermogen
Je communiceert helder en overtuigend, ook naar niet-dataspecialisten
Proactief, resultaatgericht en autonoom
Samenwerkingsgericht met oog voor het bredere organisatiedoel
Discreet en betrouwbaar in omgang met vertrouwelijke informatie
Je beweegt je vlot in een omgeving met senior stakeholders
Je spreekt zeer goed Nederlands of Frans met een goede kennis van de andere landstaal en beheerst perfect het Engels.


Waarom kiezen voor Securex?

Bij Securex draagt elke medewerker bij aan het succes van onze klanten. Daarom is het onze missie om onze medewerkers te laten schitteren in hun job! Dat begint met een sterk onboardingproces. Gedurende je hele loopbaan bieden we je de vrijheid om je persoonlijke ontwikkeling in eigen handen te nemen dankzij uitgebreide opleidingsmogelijkheden, interne mobiliteit en allerlei soorten projecten.

We bieden je een aantrekkelijk loonpakket met tal van extra voordelen, zoals maaltijdcheques, hospitalisatie-, groeps- en invaliditeitsverzekering en aantrekkelijke kortingen. Werknemers die geen bedrijfswagen hebben, kunnen ook tegen een voordelig tarief een wagen leasen. Je kan rekenen op minimaal 27 vakantiedagen in een werkweek van 38,5 uur, aangevuld met 4 extra verlofdagen (of flexibele werktijden) en 4 verlofdagen op basis van anciënniteit.

Met onze 'New Way Of Working' positioneren we ons als een flexibele werkgever met een dynamische werkomgeving. Als je functie het toelaat, kan je werken op kantoor combineren met thuiswerken. Al onze medewerkers delen een passie voor onze klanten, bouwen positieve relaties op, nemen verantwoordelijkheid en werken met een digitale mindset. We zetten ons volledig in voor maatschappelijk verantwoord ondernemen. Kiezen voor Securex betekent kiezen voor een duurzame werkgever, onderneming en partner.

Title: Design & Utilization - Senior Data Analyst
Description:
bpost strives to create an inclusive environment with challenging projects and inspiring collaborations. With our colleagues at the heart of our organisation, we believe in careers rather than jobs. As part of the international group bnode, bpost has transitioned from the Belgian postal operator to a regional and digital expert in parcel size logistics. With a focus on social and environmental sustainability, our people are the foundation of everything we do. We foster a culture that supports diversity, inclusion and career growth, ensuring that every team member has the opportunity to thrive and succeedWhat will you do?

Bnode strives to create an inclusive environment with challenging projects and inspiring collaborations. With our employees at the heart of our organization, we don’t believe in jobs, we believe in careers. We're now looking for a Senior Data Analyst who will make us move.

Your mission

The Out of Home (OOH) team within bpost is responsible for all manned and unmanned points within bpost, excluding post offices. This includes parcel points, postal points and parcel lockers. Currently bpost has around 2,500 parcel lockers and this network represents a key strategic pillar.

To support this growth, we are looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join our team. In this role, you will play a key role in driving data initiatives, building dashboards, delivering actionable insights and supporting strategic decision-making across the business.

Reporting


Take full ownership of end-to-end dashboard development: from data collection and transformation to data modelling and visualization (PowerBI), including building and maintaining Python scripts for data processing, simulation and optimization
Design, build and maintain robust data model to ensure scalable and business-ready reportings
Develop a deep understanding of system architecture and data dependencies across operational and IT environments
Act as a bridge between business and IT teams, translating business needs into technical/data requirements, and interact effectively with Central Data Team


Data analysis and profitability insights


Perform in-depth analyses on the usage of lockers, also focusing on how they contribute to profitability. You ensure clear, data-driven insights that improve capacity usage and operational performance in the long term
Prepare reports summarizing financial and operational performance, presenting key KPIs for Management
Monitor and analyze real-time data for lockers and manned points, with recommendations for adjustments and improvements


Data Modeling & Governance


Contribute to the definition and implementation of a structured and scalable data model for OOH activities
Identify data inconsistencies and actively participate in improving overall data quality
Define, implement and monitor data quality (DQ) rules in collaboration with IT and the Central Data Team
Clarify KPI definitions and ensure alignment between business and reporting logic
Support the progressive transition from manual Excel-based processes to more automated and reliable data solutions


Capacity forecasting and optimization


Collaborate with the team to make data-driven forecasts about capacity needs based on historical data, seasonal trends, and expected peak moments
Develop strategies for optimal use of lockers and manned points, focusing on balancing customer satisfaction and costs


Who are you?


University degree
Mandatory: advanced proficiency in Excel, PowerBi and Python
At least 5 years of relevant work experience in data analytics, business intelligence or any related analytical role
Excellent command of English, proficiency in Dutch and/or French is a plus
Strong affinity with capacity planning and demand forecasting techniques
Preferred: experience or strong affinity with Capacity Management/Yield Management
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to translate data into actionable insights
Proactive attitude and great problem-solving abilities
Knowledge of data modeling principles
Experience in data quality management and governance practices
Ability to work in low-maturity data environments and structure data processes


What do we offer?

Like a Long-awaited Parcel, We Want To Make You Feel Welcome And Valued. Our Offer Includes a Competitive Monthly Salary, That Goes Without Saying. On Top Of That, You Can Count On


Meal vouchers of €8 per working day
Hospitalization-, group- and disability insurances
A phone subscription, including 25GB data
A flexible renumeration plan, which allows you to customize your own benefits. A company car, bike leasing, public transportation, extra days off,... the choice is yours
Possibility to enter the Federal Mobility plan
Work/life balance, thanks to flexible working hours and the possibility to work from home
20 days of statutory leave and 7 additional extralegal days off
An end-of-year and performance-based bonus and double holiday pay
Many benefits from more than 100 bpost-partners


#bpost #bnode

Title: Demand Planner Belux
Description:
[Please note this is a Direct Search led by Bacardi, Applications from agencies will not be accepted nor will fees be paid for unsolicited CVs.]

About The Role

Join a dynamic team that provides consumer and influencer data, perspectives, and insights to guide smarter business decisions.

As a Market Demand Planner for BeLux, you will be responsible for building the most accurate and realistic market forecasts, acting as a backbone for the business to improve customer satisfaction, drive profitability, and enable a lean end-to-end supply chain.

You will work closely with cross-functional stakeholders to deeply understand the market, enrich demand signals from the regional Centre of Excellence (CoE), and challenge forecasts using strong data-driven insights.

About Bacardi

Bacardi is a vibrant, family-owned spirits company, nurtured by seven generations of the Bacardí family. Founded in 1862 in Santiago de Cuba, Bacardi revolutionized the spirits industry by creating the world’s first light-bodied rum.

Today, Bacardi is the largest privately held spirits company, with a portfolio of over 200 premium brands, including BACARDI®, GREY GOOSE®, PATRÓN®, MARTINI®, DEWAR’S®, BOMBAY SAPPHIRE®, and many others.

With more than 7,000 employees worldwide—affectionately known as Primos—Bacardi fosters a culture rooted in passion, innovation, and collaboration.

Our Culture

Our culture is built around three pillars:


Fearless: We embrace agility, innovation, and are empowered to challenge and take risks.
Family: We treat each other and our communities with care, respect, and inclusiveness.
Founders: We embody an entrepreneurial spirit that drives continuous growth.


Your Key Responsibilities

Demand Forecasting


Enrich local forecasts with market insights, focusing on the short-term horizon (0–4 months)
Collaborate with sales, marketing, and finance teams to refine forecasts
Analyze market volumes and adjust forecasts by channel where necessary
Ensure robust demand plans for business unit leadership reviews
Track KPIs and drive continuous improvement initiatives
Support Portfolio & Product Lifecycle Management with local insights
Maintain high-quality historical data to improve predictive analytics


Collaboration & Communication


Act as the voice of demand within the business unit
Work closely with supply chain, production, marketing, and sales teams
Lead demand reviews within the S&OP (Sales & Operations Planning) process
Communicate forecast changes, risks, and opportunities clearly
Coordinate with CoE demand planners on exceptions and major impacts


Promotion & Event Planning


Integrate promotions, campaigns, and market events into forecasts
Measure and analyze uplift from promotional activities


About You

You bring experience in forecasting, supply chain, and business partnering, along with strong analytical capabilities and expertise in data tools.

Skills & Experience

Analytical Thinking


Ability to interpret complex datasets
Make data-driven decisions


Demand Planning Expertise


Knowledge of forecasting methods (e.g., time series, regression)
Experience with tools such as SAP BI, IBP, or HANA


Business Acumen


Understanding of supply chain and inventory principles
Knowledge of sales, marketing, and financial drivers of demand


Collaboration & Communication


Strong cross-functional collaboration skills
Excellent communication and presentation abilities
Ability to influence stakeholders and build consensus


Problem-Solving & Adaptability


Quick decision-making in dynamic environments
Flexibility to adjust forecasts based on new inputs
Continuous improvement mindset


Technical Skills


Advanced Excel
Experience with ERP systems and visualization tools (e.g., Power BI)


Languages


Fluent English (required)


Personal Qualities


Clear and engaging communicator, able to simplify complex insights
Strategic, creative, and solutions-oriented thinker
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to translate data into action
Open to feedback, transparent, and results-driven
Collaborative team player who inspires and motivates others


LEADERSHIP AGILITY

At Bacardi we care deeply about finding brilliant talent who can bring the following personal attributes to help elevate our 3Fs: Fearless, Family, Founder culture to the next level.


Belonging: You have a strong capacity to create a culture of ‘Belonging’, where people feel appreciated for who they are, what they do, and who they can become
Self-Awareness: You have high levels of self-awareness, are reflective and know yourself well, understanding both your own strengths and weaknesses and your impact on others
People Agility: You bring a deep ability to understand and relate to people and navigate through tough situations to harness and multiply collective performance
Results Agility: You demonstrate consistent delivery of results in first-time situations by inspiring teams, exhibiting a presence that builds confidence in yourself and others.
Mental Agility: You can think critically to penetrate complex problems and expand the possibilities by making fresh connections. You think strategically whilst being execution focused and bring creativity to the challenges you’re presented with
Change Agility: You have a deep natural curiosity, desire to experiment and ability to effectively deal with the heat of change. You have a growth and learning mindset: ready to test, stumble, learn and apply from experience


Disclaimer: Bacardi seeks talent from all backgrounds to bring diversity of thought, agility and capability to our organization across the globe. We promote a working environment where all employees are treated and rewarded fairly. We do not tolerate any form of discrimination that adversely affects individuals or groups on the basis of national origin, race, color, religion, sex, gender, sexual orientation, marital status, disability, age, and any other legally protected aspect of a person’s identity.

U.S. applicants have rights under Federal Employment Laws.

Perks of Working for us

At Bacardi, we are committed to providing our Primos (employees) with a comprehensive Total Rewards package of compensation, benefits, incentives, allowances, well-being, and much more to meet the needs of themselves and their families. It goes beyond just Cash! The total rewards program is based on pay for performance, whereby the incentive programs are linked to company and individual performance.

Compensation, Paid Time Off & Retirement:


Competitive Pay Package
Competitive Holiday/Paid Time Off, plus additional days offered ( i.e., volunteer day, allowing you to give back to the community)
Retirement/Pension Plan


Health & Wellbeing


Medical, Critical Illness, and Life Insurance
Employee Assistance Programs
Best-in-class, family-friendly, and inclusive leave policies


Additional Benefits

The benefits /perks may vary depending on the nature of your employment with Bacardi, your work location, and are subject to change.

Some benefits may be subject to an employee contribution.

Some benefits may be fully or partially subsidized. Incentive plans are linked to the nature of your employment and can vary based on region, market, function or brand.

Title: Sales Performance Analyst
Description:
We are looking for a Senior Sales Performance Analyst who combines strong analytical thinking with the ability to quickly translate insights into concrete actions. In this role, you will turn data into meaningful recommendations and help design incentive schemes that truly drive performance within our commercial organization.

Your Responsibilities


Analyze sales performance data, structures, and go‑to‑market models.
Understand and synthesize key insights quickly, without getting lost in details.
Manage the commission scheme, including calculation, validation, and communication to sales teams.
Develop or optimize incentive and bonus schemes together with Sales Excellence and the commercial field.
Supports the development of automated tools and financial models to increase efficiency and accuracy of analyses and reporting
Evaluate the impact of initiatives and recommend adjustments to increase effectiveness.
Provide insights and explanations to the Controlling department when needed
Give recommendations on how to improve sales performance through data analysis and insights
Collaborate closely with Sales, Finance, and Controlling to support decision-making.


Your profile


Strong analytical skills, with the right balance between detailed understanding and taking swift actions.
Excellent command of Excel or/and Power BI with willingness to learn Power BI; familiarity with more advanced analytical tools is a plus.
Fluent in Dutch or French, with good command of English.
Preferred backgrounds include but not limited to : consultancy, entrepreneurship, business/sales analysis, companies with a strong sales culture.
Natural high attention to detail while maintaining focus and pace
You are an excellent communicator, both in word and writing
You have experience in working cross-functional and positively influencing as needed
Experience working with incentive or bonus systems is a strong advantage.
Organized, proactive, solution‑oriented, and a quick learner.


What can you expect from us?


A competitive salary complemented by fringe benefits such as company car, meal vouchers, eco vouchers, hospitalization insurance, and group insurance
Sector-specific bonuses
6 extra leave days (ADV)
The chance to be part of a growing, purpose-driven team that fosters collaboration and continuous improvement
A role where your contribution directly impacts customer satisfaction and company success
A supportive environment where your ideas and insights are encouraged and appreciated

Title: Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Developer
Description:
Title: Microsoft Fabric & Power BI Developer

Location: Brussels, Belgium (Hybrid)

Employment Contract.




Job Description:

Technical Expertise:

- Strong experience with Microsoft Fabric (Lakehouse, Warehouse, Dataflows Gen2)

- Advanced Power BI & DAX modeling expertise

- Proficiency in SQL, Power Query (M), and PySpark/Python

- Experience with Git-based version control and CI/CD processes




Experience:

- 6+ years in Business Intelligence / Data Engineering / Analytics

- 5+ years working with Power BI (including data modeling)

- Hands-on experience with modern cloud data platforms (Fabric/Azure)







Responsibility:

Analytics Platform & DevOps:

- Design and maintain Fabric deployment pipelines across Dev, UAT, and Production environments

- Implement CI/CD practices using Git (Azure DevOps or GitHub)

- Automate environment parameterization (data sources, Lakehouses, endpoints)

- Define and enforce development standards, governance, and documentation




Data Engineering (Microsoft Fabric):

- Architect scalable data platforms using Medallion architecture (Bronze / Silver / Gold)

- Build and orchestrate data ingestion pipelines (Data Factory, Dataflows Gen2)

- Develop transformations using PySpark, SQL, and Power Query

- Integrate data from enterprise systems (e.g., SAP, Azure SQL, APIs, Snowflake)




Semantic Modeling & Power BI:

- Design enterprise semantic models using star schema principles

- Develop and optimize complex DAX calculations for financial and analytical use cases

- Implement high-performance architectures (e.g., Direct Lake, optimized import models)

- Utilize advanced tools (Tabular Editor, DAX Studio, ALM Toolkit) for model governance




Performance, Security & User Experience:

- Implement Row-Level Security (RLS) and data access frameworks

- Optimize end-to-end performance across datasets and reports

- Deliver executive-grade dashboards with strong UX and data storytelling principles

- Ensure high standards of data quality, reliability, and usability

Required Skills & Experience







Nice to Have:

- Microsoft Certification (e.g., DP-600 Fabric Analytics Engineer)

- Experience in Finance, FP&A, or regulated environments

Key Competencies:

- Strong architectural mindset with focus on scalability and governance

- Ability to translate business needs into data solutions

- Autonomous, proactive, and comfortable working with senior stakeholders

- Focus on quality, performance, and continuous improvement.

Title: Business & Regulatory Reporting analyst
Description:
Business & Regulatory Reporting Analyst

Auderghem, Belgium – Hybrid




Edenred PayTech EU is a financial institution authorized under an Electronic Money license and regulated by the National Bank of Belgium (NBB). As part of the global Edenred Group, our mission is to provide innovative payment and transactional solutions across a wide range of services, including card payment issuing, transaction processing, and banking-as-a-service. Rooted in technology and digital expertise, we empower our partners and customers with reliable and scalable solutions.




As the Business & Regulatory Reporting Analyst, you will develop, implement and maintain a robust approach to data quality and compliance. You will analyze the latest regulations affecting electronic payment institutions and ensure we meet all necessary reporting requirements (e.g. AMLA, CESOP, FICOBA, PCC, PSD3, SEPA).




As you settle in this role, you will build a strong understanding of our business, products, and the PSD3 framework, along with their impact on reporting. You will have a keen interest in the constantly evolving payment landscape and regulatory developments.




Eventually, you will take charge of analyzing data for various business reports and contribute in interpreting trends, predicting patterns, and improving existing processes.




The Business & Regulatory Reporting Analyst works as part of the MIS and regulatory reporting team. You will liaise with the whole organisation, whether as the source of data or as users of the reports produced by the team.




What you will be doing:




Your responsibilities will be split into two main groups:




Regulatory Reporting

Prepare, review, and validate regulatory reports prior to submission.
Compile data to produce accurate, timely, and complete submissions.
Investigate queries raised by supervisory authorities and ensure appropriate follow-up.
Ensure overall completeness, accuracy, and quality of the reported data.




Business Analysis

Maintain Edenred PayTech EU’s reporting framework, identify new requirements, and track updates and corrections to regulatory reporting systems and data warehouses.
Keep internal documentation and manuals up to date as needed.
Promote the integrity of Edenred PayTech EU across its various stakeholders considering quality, accuracy, and timeliness.
Produce reports as requested and deliver clear, timely feedback on related matters.

These responsibilities represent the core functions of the position and may evolve in line with business needs and role progression.




Key Working Relationships




Internal: You will work in close collaboration with our departments in the UK (e.g. Data, MIS) and local teams in Brussels (e.g. AML, Operations, Data).




External: You would liaise with different competent authorities as The National Bank of Belgium or l’Autorité de Contrôle Prudentiel et de Résolution, as well as other obliged stakeholders, including agents, third-party suppliers, and different financial institutions.




What you will bring:




Required:




Bachelor/Master’s in Finance, Business Sciences, Mathematics, Statistics or a related field. A combination of relevant experience and professional achievements may be considered as a substitute of a Bachelor/Master's Degree
Strong analytical skills, with the ability to challenge existing processes and propose alternative solutions.
Familiarity with SQL and MS Excel to process large amounts of data files efficiently and turn data into clear reports.
Self-motivated and results-oriented, with the ability to take initiative when needed.
Strong verbal and written communication skills to convey information clearly to different audiences.
Languages: Full working knowledge of English. Fluency in French is a nice-to-have.




Nice to Have:




Prior experience in regulatory or risk reporting for a financial institution.
Basic to advanced knowledge of financial regulations (e.g. AMLA, CESOP, GDPR, PSD3, EBA Guidelines) is highly desired.
Understanding of ‘Customer Due Diligence’ and ‘Know Your Customer’ processes.
Basic programming experience (e.g. Python, DAX, VBA, etc).
Exposure to visualization tools (e.g. Power BI, AWS, Databricks, etc).

Why Edenred PayTech EU?




We are a subsidiary of the Edenred group and leaders in prepaid solutions including banking, virtual cards, debit, credit, and prepaid processing.




As an Edenred PayTech EU employee you will get the support and structure that you need to enjoy your work and develop your career while doing what you love and making a difference in a fast-paced and innovative business.




Diversity:




Edenred PayTech EU are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We will not discriminate against any applicant or employee based on age, race, colour, creed, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, national origin, citizenship, marital status or civil partnership/union status, disability, pregnancy, genetic information, or any other basis prohibited by applicable country or local laws.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
An exciting opportunity for a skilled Data Scientist to join a dynamic team focused on advancing the field of biopharmaceutical manufacturing. This role will involve collaborating with various functions within the organization to drive innovation and excellence in data science applications related to drug substance manufacturing processes. You will play a crucial role in supporting the development and optimization of key processes and methodologies.

Responsibilities


Provide advanced statistical and data science support across multiple functions, including cell culture, purification, and analytical activities.
Engage in process optimization, investigation, comparability studies, monitoring, method transfer, validation, and characterization.
Contribute to the preparation of protocols and reports with significant statistical content through data collection, experiment design, and data analysis.
Ensure the traceability and correctness of collected data, facilitating timely information exchange with relevant stakeholders.
Maintain compliance with legal standards and regulations, ensuring high-quality scientific and technical documentation.



Technical Skills


Proficient in statistical data analysis and data science techniques.
Experience working in a cGMP environment, with a strong understanding of regulatory guidelines.
Domain knowledge in biologics, particularly in upstream or downstream development and manufacturing.
Ability to lead complex technical projects effectively.
Strong problem-solving skills with a change agent mentality to propose innovative solutions to scientific challenges.



Profile


Holds a Bachelor's degree in data sciences, statistics, or a related discipline with a minimum of 6 years of experience in the pharmaceutical industry; or a Master's degree with at least 4 years of experience; or a Ph.D. with a minimum of 2 years of experience.
Demonstrates effective communication skills, both written and verbal, for cross-functional collaboration, particularly in English. Proficiency in Dutch is advantageous.
A team player capable of working autonomously in a collaborative, dynamic environment.



Practical Information


This position is based at a biopharmaceutical manufacturing site near Geel.
Fluent English is required, and proficiency in Dutch is a plus.
This is a long-term opportunity available for both consultants on our payroll and freelance professionals.



Contactperson & Reference


Reference #: INCA26907
Casper Moyaert
casper.moyaert@i4m.be

Title: Data Analyst Junior
Description:
Position : Data Analyst Junior

Location: Canton of Vaud, Switzerland

Workload: 100%

Start date: To be defined




Context




Our client, a major international organisation, is looking for a Junior Data Analyst to strengthen its medical team as part of the development and operation of a new medical platform.

This platform aims to centralise, structure and improve the reliability of medical data, particularly data related to injuries, in order to support reporting, analysis and continuous improvement of medical processes.

The position is part of an environment combining healthcare, data quality and digital platform operations, within a dynamic and specialised team.




Purpose of the Role




The Junior Data Analyst will contribute to the quality, consistency and reliability of the data integrated into the medical platform.

They will support data collection, validation, cleaning and documentation activities to ensure that the information used by the medical teams is complete, usable and compliant with defined standards.




Key Responsibilities




Support data collection and data flow monitoring related to the medical platform.
Perform regular validation, cleaning and consistency checks on incoming data.
Identify missing, inconsistent, incomplete or implausible data.
Follow up with the relevant colleagues or contributors to resolve data anomalies.
Contribute to the documentation of field definitions, coding rules, procedures and data-entry standards.
Prepare clean and usable datasets for senior colleagues within the medical unit.
Support functional testing of the platform, particularly regarding data entry, validation logic and output quality.
Contribute to the continuous improvement of data management and data quality processes.
Promote a culture of rigour, quality and responsibility around medical data.




Candidate Profile




Bachelor’s degree required in a relevant field such as health sciences, epidemiology, data science, biostatistics or a related discipline.
Master’s degree would be an advantage but is not mandatory.
Basic knowledge of human anatomy and musculoskeletal injuries.
Strong interest in data quality, validity and structuring.
Organised, reliable and rigorous profile with strong attention to detail.
Good communication skills to follow up on inconsistencies or missing data.
Motivation to work in a medical environment and contribute to the development of a digital platform.




Expected Experience




Initial experience gained through studies, internships, student jobs or early professional experience.
Experience in data handling, data administration or support for research activities.
Experience with structured datasets or data quality control processes would be an asset.
Exposure to a medical, healthcare or research environment would be appreciated.




Key Skills




Excellent attention to detail.
Strong sense of rigour and reliability.
Ability to consistently follow defined processes.
Good coordination and communication skills.
Proactive and service-oriented mindset.
Ability to work collaboratively in a dynamic team environment.
Willingness to learn and contribute to a platform development project.




Technical Skills




Good command of Microsoft Office, particularly Excel, Word and Outlook.
Initial experience with a data-related tool or environment would be an asset: advanced Excel, SPSS, R, Python, SQL or Tableau.
Ability to work with structured templates, data-entry systems and functional documentation.




Languages




Fluent English required, both written and spoken.
French and/or German would be an asset.







🌍 Founded in 2007, Antaes is a Swiss management and technology consulting company 🇨🇭, ranked among the top 10 consulting firms in Switzerland. We have over 300 experienced engineers who share our passion.




📍 With offices in Switzerland, Singapore, Hong Kong, and France, we support our Swiss and international clients in the following areas:

Organisation & Transformation Consulting
Industrial Engineering
Information Systems Management




By joining our team, you will discover:

A dynamic team with a start-up spirit
A human-centric approach & personalized career follow-up
Education & Training to keep developing your skills
Exciting events: team building, meet-ups, workshops, afterworks, and Winter + Summer Events

🏆 A company certified @HappyAtWork and committed to CSR (Ecovadis 2025 Gold Medal 🥇)




You too can be part of the Antaes adventure !

Title: Data Analyst - BI & Automation
Description:
Do you enjoy working with data, systems, dashboards, and business insights? Are you comfortable connecting different data sources, checking accuracy, and helping teams make better decisions? Do you like creating structure where data flows are complex? Then this role is for you.

We are looking for a detail-oriented and technically skilled Data Analyst to join our international team. In this role, you will build and improve reporting processes, dashboards, and analytical tools, working closely with finance, management, operations, and technical teams to ensure business data is accurate, consistent, well-structured, and transformed into useful insights for decision-making.

In this role, you will act as a key link between business stakeholders and technical teams, helping translate operational, financial, and management needs into reliable reports, dashboards, data flows, and analytical tools.

 

About PHILIPP PLEIN

PHILIPP PLEIN is an international luxury fashion and lifestyle group headquartered in Switzerland, operating across multiple brands and global markets. Known for bold creativity, innovation, and a strong international presence, the company continues to grow through retail, strategic partnerships, and digital commerce.

As we continue to strengthen our data-driven operations, this role will play an important part in improving visibility, accuracy, reporting quality, and decision-making across finance, operations, management, and key business teams.

 

Key Responsibilities

Build, maintain, and improve reports, dashboards, and analytical tools.
Work with databases, APIs, internal systems, and external data sources.
Extract, clean, map, transform, and validate data from multiple sources.
Monitor data connections, data flows, and reporting accuracy.
Identify data inconsistencies, investigate root causes, and suggest corrections.
Support finance with reporting, analysis, reconciliation, and performance tracking.
Create data visualizations using BI tools or custom-built dashboard solutions.
Automate reporting processes where possible.
Document data logic, report structures, sources, mappings, and calculations.
Translate business needs into clear reporting and data requirements.

 

Required Skills & Experience

Experience in data analysis, business analysis, reporting, BI, or financial analysis.
Strong SQL skills and experience working with databases.
Good knowledge of Python for data analysis, including pandas or similar libraries.
Experience with APIs, data integrations, data mapping, and multiple data sources.
Ability to design and build custom dashboards or internal reporting tools.
Strong Excel skills.
Understanding of data cleaning, validation, transformation, and accuracy checks.
Experience with financial or business performance data.
Experience with BI tools such as Power BI, Tableau, Looker Studio, or similar.
Knowledge of ETL processes, data pipelines, or reporting automation.
Ability to communicate clearly with finance, management, and technical teams.

 

Nice to Have

Experience with ERP, CRM, accounting, or operational systems.
Microsoft Power Automate experience.
Experience with AI tools such as Claude Code or Microsoft Copilot.

 

Ideal Profile

Analytical, precise, and highly detail-oriented.
Comfortable working with raw and imperfect data.
Able to check, challenge, and verify numbers.
Proactive in finding issues and improving reporting processes.
Business-minded and able to explain data clearly to non-technical users.

HTML:
Do you enjoy working with data, systems, dashboards, and business insights? Are you comfortable connecting different data sources, checking accuracy, and helping teams make better decisions? Do you like creating structure where data flows are complex? Then this role is for you.

We are looking for a detail-oriented and technically skilled Data Analyst to join our international team. In this role, you will build and improve reporting processes, dashboards, and analytical tools, working closely with finance, management, operations, and technical teams to ensure business data is accurate, consistent, well-structured, and transformed into useful insights for decision-making.

In this role, you will act as a key link between business stakeholders and technical teams, helping translate operational, financial, and management needs into reliable reports, dashboards, data flows, and analytical tools.

 

About PHILIPP PLEIN

PHILIPP PLEIN is an international luxury fashion and lifestyle group headquartered in Switzerland, operating across multiple brands and global markets. Known for bold creativity, innovation, and a strong international presence, the company continues to grow through retail, strategic partnerships, and digital commerce.

As we continue to strengthen our data-driven operations, this role will play an important part in improving visibility, accuracy, reporting quality, and decision-making across finance, operations, management, and key business teams.

 

Key Responsibilities

  • Build, maintain, and improve reports, dashboards, and analytical tools.
  • Work with databases, APIs, internal systems, and external data sources.
  • Extract, clean, map, transform, and validate data from multiple sources.
  • Monitor data connections, data flows, and reporting accuracy.
  • Identify data inconsistencies, investigate root causes, and suggest corrections.
  • Support finance with reporting, analysis, reconciliation, and performance tracking.
  • Create data visualizations using BI tools or custom-built dashboard solutions.
  • Automate reporting processes where possible.
  • Document data logic, report structures, sources, mappings, and calculations.
  • Translate business needs into clear reporting and data requirements.

 

Required Skills & Experience

  • Experience in data analysis, business analysis, reporting, BI, or financial analysis.
  • Strong SQL skills and experience working with databases.
  • Good knowledge of Python for data analysis, including pandas or similar libraries.
  • Experience with APIs, data integrations, data mapping, and multiple data sources.
  • Ability to design and build custom dashboards or internal reporting tools.
  • Strong Excel skills.
  • Understanding of data cleaning, validation, transformation, and accuracy checks.
  • Experience with financial or business performance data.
  • Experience with BI tools such as Power BI, Tableau, Looker Studio, or similar.
  • Knowledge of ETL processes, data pipelines, or reporting automation.
  • Ability to communicate clearly with finance, management, and technical teams.

 

Nice to Have

  • Experience with ERP, CRM, accounting, or operational systems.
  • Microsoft Power Automate experience.
  • Experience with AI tools such as Claude Code or Microsoft Copilot.

 

Ideal Profile

  • Analytical, precise, and highly detail-oriented.
  • Comfortable working with raw and imperfect data.
  • Able to check, challenge, and verify numbers.
  • Proactive in finding issues and improving reporting processes.
  • Business-minded and able to explain data clearly to non-technical users.
  • Independent, structured, and reliable.

Description:
About TCS:

Tata Consultancy Services is an IT services, consulting and business solutions organization that has been partnering with many of the world’s largest businesses in their transformation journeys for over 50 years. TCS offers a consulting-led, cognitive powered, integrated portfolio of business, technology and engineering services and solutions. This is delivered through its unique Location Independent Agile™ delivery model, recognized as a benchmark of excellence in software development.




TCS has been serving customers in Switzerland since 1985. The company’s presence in Switzerland consists of offices in Zurich, Basel and Nyon. TCS Switzerland offers a broad spectrum of IT Services, Consulting and Financial Solutions. TCS has over 600+ consultants working in Switzerland and over 11000 associates supporting Swiss Clients from Offshore.




About this role:

Serve as a strategic advisor to client stakeholders and technical teams.
Drive business development by identifying and pursuing opportunities to sell data analytics services to Chief Data Officers and other key decision-makers.
Develop and present compelling presentations to senior customer executives and stakeholders
Work with customer to drive workshops and create data analytics charters and business cases.
Demand Creation: Work with customers to generate data analytics project opportunities
Customer Advisory: Lead Data & Analytics Assessment, Strategy and Roadmap engagements and drive customer workshops for analytics use cases
PreSales Leadership: Own stages of the presales cycle, RFI, RFP, and contribute to the development of solutions for presales

Proven experience in a leadership role within data analytics.
Extensive experience with cloud data platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP, Snowflake, Databricks) and Lakehouse architecture.

Strong ability to plan and manage multiple initiatives simultaneously.

Title: Data Analyst (F/H/X)
Description:
Amaris recrute un Data Analyst Logistique (H/F) chargé de collecter, exploiter et interpréter les données afin de définir des orientations et optimiser nos performances logistiques. La personne retenue rejoindra nos équipes bbasées à Genève.




🔥 Vos responsabilités :

Contribuer aux projets logistiques en apportant une expertise en analyse de données.
Comprendre les enjeux métier et les traduire en problématiques analytiques, avec de fréquents déplacements sur le terrain.
Cartographier les flux et identifier les données et points de mesure pertinents.
Extraire les données depuis les systèmes sources (SAP, MES, LES, équipements).
Préparer, structurer et fiabiliser les données pour les rendre exploitables.
Réaliser des analyses de données et utiliser des modèles de simulation pour proposer des pistes d’optimisation logistique.
Concevoir des tableaux de bord et indicateurs de performance.
Présenter les résultats de manière claire grâce à des outils de data visualisation.
Assurer une veille sur les outils d’analyse et de simulation.







🎯 Votre profil

Formation d’ingénieur HES, EPF ou équivalent.
Goût prononcé pour le terrain et la collecte du besoin auprès des opérationnels.
Maîtrise des outils d’analyse de données (Power BI ou Tableau Prep, KNIME ou équivalent), de visualisation (Tableau) et des bases de données (SQL).
Excellentes capacités d’organisation et forte autonomie.
Aisance relationnelle, sens de l’écoute, capacité de synthèse et bonne communication.
Dynamisme, flexibilité et esprit d’équipe.







💡Ce que nous offrons

Une communauté internationale réunissant plus de 110 nationalités différentes, avec une forte présence en Suisse et en Europe.
Un environnement où la confiance a une place centrale : 70% de nos leaders clés ont commencé leur carrière au premier niveau de responsabilités.
Un système de formation solide avec notre Académie interne et plus de 250 modules disponibles, adaptés aux enjeux industriels suisses.
Des engagements forts en matière de RSE, notamment par le biais de notre programme WeCare Together.




Amaris Consulting s’engage à promouvoir la diversité au sein de ses effectifs et à créer un environnement de travail inclusif. Nous étudions les candidatures de toutes personnes qualifiées, indépendamment de leur sexe, orientation sexuelle, race, origine ethnique, croyances, âge, état civil, handicap ou toute autre caractéristique. Une attention particulière sera portée aux candidats en situation de handicap .




Postulez dès maintenant et rejoignez notre équipe pour une carrière enrichissante et stimulante !

Title: Data Analyst — SQL · Python · BI | Pharma Manufacturing
Description:
Recrutis is supporting one of its clients, a manufacturing technology company in the life sciences sector, in the search for a Junior Data Analyst, based in canton of Vaud, as part of a consulting mission.




Why this role

A concrete, hands-on entry-level data role where you own the full cycle — collecting, aggregating, structuring, querying, and analysing manufacturing data.

A strong first step into pharma: you work close to operations, with real datasets and real operational impact, in a defined mission with a clear scope and timeline.

A role with a genuine AI dimension: you contribute directly to building and testing AI-assisted tools that automate reporting, data processing, and knowledge management.




Key information

Location: Canton of Vaud, on-site presence.

Contract: consulting mission until end of December 2026, with a budget review in October.

Start: as soon as possible.

Seniority: A first professional experience is expected, not deep expertise.

Languages: English required; French a clear asset.




Scope

Collect, aggregate, and clean manufacturing data from multiple sources.

Build and run queries to extract and structure relevant datasets.

Analyse data and produce clear, actionable outputs for the operations teams.

Develop and maintain dashboards and reports using BI tools.

Develop and maintain AI-assisted tools to automate report generation from manufacturing databases and dashboards.

Support the implementation and testing of AI solutions for data processing, reporting, and knowledge management.




Profile

A first hands-on experience in data analysis with SQL databases.

Python, R, or equivalent scripting capability.

Working knowledge of a business intelligence tool (Power BI, Tableau, Spotfire, or equivalent).

A first professional experience in a pharma / regulated manufacturing environment.

A first exposure to, or genuine interest in, AI / machine learning, and English at professional working level (mandatory).




Strong assets — Differentiators

First exposure to manufacturing or production datasets.

Familiarity with basic data pipelines and data management practices.

French language skills, and any first exposure to AI / analytics projects.




Process — Confidential recruitment process. Response within 48 hours for applications meeting required criteria. Active mandate, priority review.




Keywords — Junior Data Analyst, SQL, Python, R, Power BI, Tableau, Spotfire, business intelligence, data management, AI-assisted analytics, manufacturing data, pharma, machine learning, Yverdon, Vaud, Switzerland




Fabrice Cattant — Founder, Recrutis | Expert & Executive Search in Life Sciences

Title: Data Analyst (Engineer)
Description:
About Revolut

People deserve more from their money. More visibility, more control, and more freedom. Since 2015, Revolut has been on a mission to deliver just that. Our powerhouse of products — including spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more — help our 75+ million customers get more from their money every day.

As we continue our lightning-fast growth,‌ 2 things are essential to our success: our people and our culture. In recognition of our outstanding employee experience, we've been certified as a Great Place to Work™. So far, we have 13,000+ people working around the world, from our offices and remotely, to help us achieve our mission. And we're looking for more brilliant people. People who love building great products, redefining success, and turning the complexity of a chaotic world into the simplicity of a beautiful solution.

About The Role

Our Data Science team solves complex problems with smart, practical solutions. Data Scientists and Analysts work directly with product teams to uncover insights, guide decisions, and improve how customers experience Revolut. They do this by delivering smart, scalable solutions that move the business forward.

We’re looking for a Data Analyst who can dig into our complex databases, look for the root cause of a problem, and design their own solutions by writing code to implement them.

This is an opportunity to apply your skills while picking up new ones and delivering value for millions of customers around the world.

Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch.

What You'll Be Doing


Understanding our business and its processes through data
Applying this understanding and knowledge of data to help product and service teams
Developing documentation and data governance
Owning the entire ETL process
Designing key metrics to measure different aspects of the business
Creating and maintaining new aggregated views and tables to simplify data querying
Providing clean data sets, modelling data in a way that empowers end users to answer their own questions


What You'll Need


4+ years of experience in an analytical role, creating impactful data-driven solutions
A solid background/education in a quantitative discipline
Great skills with Python, SQL, or other programming languages
Evidence of exceptional mathematical and statistics knowledge
Fluency in English


Nice to have


An advanced degree in a core STEM subject
Experience with additional programming languages (Java, Scala, C++, etc.)
Knowledge of financial concepts/data
Fluency in French, German, or Italian


Building a global financial super app isn’t enough. Our Revoluters are a priority, and that’s why in 2021 we launched our inaugural D&I Framework, designed to help us thrive and grow everyday. We're not just doing this because it's the right thing to do. We’re doing it because we know that seeking out diverse talent and creating an inclusive workplace is the way to create exceptional, innovative products and services for our customers. That’s why we encourage applications from people with diverse backgrounds and experiences to join this multicultural, hard-working team.

Important notice for candidates:

Job scams are on the rise. Please keep these guidelines in mind when applying for any open roles.


Only apply through official Revolut channels. We don’t use any third-party services or platforms for our recruitment.
Always double-check the emails you receive. Make sure all communications are being done through official Revolut emails, with an @revolut.com domain.


We won't ask for payment or personal financial information during the hiring process. If anyone does ask you for this, it’s a scam. Report it immediately.

By submitting this application, I confirm that all the information given by me in this application for employment and any additional documents attached hereto are true to the best of my knowledge and that I have not wilfully suppressed any material fact. I confirm I have disclosed if applicable any previous employment with Revolut. I accept that if any of the information given by me in this application is in any way false or incorrect, my application may be rejected, any offer of employment may be withdrawn or my employment with Revolut may be terminated summarily or I may be dismissed. By submitting this application, I agree that my personal data will be processed in accordance with Revolut's Candidate Privacy Notice

Title: Stagiaire Data Analyst
Description:
Description de l'entreprise

Le présent et le futur d’Audemars Piguet se construisent grâce à la contribution de tous nos talents. Inspirés par la richesse de notre passé, nous sommes enthousiastes à l’idée de ce que nous créerons dans le futur. Ensemble, nous sommes résolument tournés vers l’avenir et en quête continue d’excellence dans tous nos métiers.

Si notre mission vous inspire, venez poursuivre votre parcours au sein de notre famille pour que l’on continue de créer l'extraordinaire. Écrivons le prochain chapitre de votre carrière ensemble !

Description du poste

Dans le cadre d’un projet stratégique de digitalisation de nos ateliers de production, nous proposons un stage de 6 mois, à partir de septembre 2026, centré sur la collecte et l’exploitation des données issues des moyens de mesure utilisés dans le flux de production horloger. Le projet s’inscrit au cœur de problématiques industrielles concrètes et aura un impact direct sur la robustesse et la performance de nos processus.

Dans ce cadre, vous serez en charge des principales tâches suivantes :


Structurer et consolider les données provenant de nos ateliers de production
Développer des outils analytiques permettant de créer de la valeur et de sécuriser notre production
Mettre en application ces outils au sein de notre production afin de proposer des réglages initiaux optimisés


Qualifications


Étudiant·e de Master en Data Science, Microtechnique, Génie mécanique, Informatique, ou domaine apparenté
Connaissances approfondies en programmation (Python ou MATLAB)
Maîtrise de MS office
Intérêt pour les systèmes de mesure et/ou la mécanique horlogère
Capacité à travailler de manière rigoureuse, structurée et méthodique
Esprit d’équipe, sens du terrain et motivation pour interagir de manière transverse
