Title: BI & Strategic Analytics Lead (m/f/d)
Description:
About Hilo By Aktiia

High blood pressure is the world's most common disease, causing 18M deaths each year. At Hilo by Aktiia, our vision is a world where no lives are lost or damaged from the effects of high blood pressure. Our mission is to make this a reality by developing tech to help people control their blood pressure.

We are a venture-backed scale-up company, having raised over $96M from investors in Europe and the United States. Our technology - rooted in 18 years of research at the Swiss Center for Electronics and Microtechnology (CSEM) - is the world's only medically accurate, continuous blood pressure monitor that is cuffless in daily life in the consumer space. Validated through extensive clinical trials and CE Marked as a Class IIa medical device, our solution is now available in 12 countries. We are a hybrid/remote-first company, with Headquarters in Neuchâtel, Switzerland, united by our passion for impact and innovation.

Position Summary

The Business Intelligence & Strategic Analytics Lead is responsible for defining and executing Hilo's Business Intelligence strategy, enabling data-informed decision making across the organisation. Acting as a strategic partner to Finance, Product, Commercial, Clinical and Leadership teams, this role translates complex business challenges into scalable analytics solutions and actionable insights. You will own the evolution of Hilo's BI capability, driving reporting excellence, data governance and self-service analytics while helping shape key business decisions through trusted data.

Core Responsibilities

Analytics & Reporting

Design, develop and continuously evolve dashboards, KPI frameworks and self-service analytics capabilities that empower teams to make confident, data-informed decisions.


Deliver timely, accurate and actionable insights that support operational and strategic decision making.
Collaborate with business leaders to identify analytical opportunities and define reporting requirements.
Drive adoption and continuous improvement of reporting and analytics tools across the organisation.
Monitor and enhance reporting performance, usability, and stakeholder satisfaction.


BI Strategy & Leadership


Define and execute Hilo's Business Intelligence strategy and roadmap, ensuring alignment with company objectives and priorities.
Own, maintain, and continuously evolve Hilo's BI platform (Metabase) as the central source of truth for operational, commercial, product, and financial reporting.
Establish BI standards, governance frameworks, and best practices to ensure consistency, scalability, and reliability across analytics solutions.
Champion a data-driven culture across the organisation by promoting the effective use of data in decision-making.
Prioritise BI initiatives based on business value, strategic impact and organisational priorities to maximise measurable outcomes.
Act as a trusted analytics partner to Finance, Product, Commercial, Clinical, and Leadership teams.
Own end-to-end analytics initiatives, aligning stakeholders, ensuring delivery excellence and driving measurable business impact.


Business Performance & Strategic Analytics


Partner with the Operations to analyse customer acquisition, onboarding, engagement, retention, churn, monetisation, and lifetime value across Hilo's product portfolio.
Deliver actionable insights that inform commercial strategy, resource allocation, pricing decisions, and business prioritisation.
Partner with leadership on strategic analyses, forecasting, scenario modelling and performance reviews to support high-impact business decisions.
Identify opportunities to improve operational efficiency and business outcomes through data-driven recommendations.


Data Management & Governance


Establish and maintain high standards for data quality, consistency and reliability across all BI and analytics solutions.
Define and maintain business metrics, KPI definitions, and data standards.
Partner with Data Engineering and IT teams to support data architecture, integration, and scalability.
Champion data governance initiatives, including security, compliance, documentation, and metric standardisation.


Stakeholder Management & Project Delivery


Act as the trusted Business Intelligence partner to Finance, Product, Commercial, Clinical and Leadership teams, translating business needs into impactful analytics solutions.
Lead BI initiatives end-to-end, from requirements gathering through design, development and deployment, ensuring high-quality and timely delivery.
Align stakeholders, manage priorities and balance competing business needs to maximise business impact.
Present insights and recommendations to senior leadership through clear data storytelling, enabling informed strategic decisions.
Ensure BI solutions are scalable, maintainable and aligned with Hilo's broader data architecture and governance standards.


Requirements

Qualifications


Proven experience leading Business Intelligence, Analytics, or Data Management initiatives in a fast-paced, data-driven environment.
Bachelor's degree in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Information Systems, Statistics, Business, or a related field.
Proven experience building scalable Business Intelligence solutions using platforms such as Metabase, Power BI, Tableau or similar.
Proficiency in SQL and data modeling concepts.
Experience working with data warehouses and cloud platforms (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, Azure Synapse, etc.).
Strong understanding of ETL/ELT processes and data pipelines.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.


Preferred Qualifications


Master's degree in a relevant field.
Experience with modern data stack technologies.
Knowledge of Python or R for advanced analytics.
Experience in Agile project management environments.
Relevant certifications (Metabase, Power BI, Tableau, Snowflake, Azure, AWS, etc.).


Success Metrics


High adoption and effective use of BI solutions across the organisation.
Trusted, accurate and consistent business reporting.
Increased self-service analytics and reduced manual reporting.
Strong stakeholder satisfaction and executive confidence in analytics.
Successful delivery of BI initiatives on time and with measurable business impact.
Clear contribution of analytics to commercial performance and strategic decision making.


Benefits

What Makes You A Great Fit

At Hilo by Aktiia, our values guide everything we do:

Move the Needle


You focus on what truly drives impact for our customers and company
You use data and clarity to guide your decisions and prioritize what matters most
You push boundaries with bold, thoughtful innovation and hold yourself accountable for results


Move Fast, Learn, Refine


You work with urgency and purpose, balancing speed with high quality
You treat mistakes as learning opportunities, experiment thoughtfully, and iterate quickly
You take ownership, act hands-on, and always look for smarter, faster ways to get things done - including leveraging AI and new tools


Team First


You put the team above individual interests, building trust through openness and respect
You encourage healthy debate, challenge directly while caring personally, and stand behind team decisions
You act as an owner, stepping in when needed and doing what's necessary for the team's success


Why Join Us?


Hilo by Aktiia is becoming one of the most important med-tech companies, and you can be part of this exciting story
Work on a mission that matters: transforming cardiovascular health at global scale
Be part of a high-impact, fast-growing, and venture-backed scale-up company
Collaborate with a diverse, passionate, and talented team
Competitive compensation and equity program, and other benefits (depending on location)


Ready to Move the Needle?

If you are passionate about making a meaningful impact in people's lives and excel in an environment where individuals take ownership and are committed to delivering results, we are happy to receive your application.

Title: Data & Marketing Analyst
Description:
At A-Players, we are building a new AI product in the mental health and mindfulness space - and we are looking for a Data & Marketing Analyst who will own the analytics layer from day one.

We are not building analytics after the fact. We are building it before the first ad goes live. That means clean tracking from day one, attribution that reflects reality, and dashboards the performance team opens every morning - not once a quarter.

In practice, you will set up the full tracking infrastructure from scratch, own the attribution model, connect marketing spend to real revenue, and keep LTV and retention metrics current and trustworthy. You will work side by side with the performance marketing team.

You will be the dedicated analyst on this product. Around you is a mature data team of 10+ people across Data Analytics, Data Engineering, Data Science, BI, and AI, as well as shared infrastructure including BigQuery, dbt, Tableau, and GA4.

This role is a fit if you have navigated the full analytics stack at a fast-moving web subscription product, know what it takes to build trust in data from scratch, and want to do meaningful work on something that is just getting started.

Responsibilities


Build the web tracking foundation for the product. Set up GA4 and GTM, both client-side and server-side, from scratch: UTM conventions, dataLayer schema, event deduplication, and attribution windows.
Implement server-side conversions for paid channels, including Meta CAPI, Google Enhanced Conversions, and TikTok Events API.
Build and maintain a web attribution model that connects marketing spend to actual revenue, and deliver channel-level LTV and CAC analysis.
Design and own Tableau dashboards for acquisition metrics, including CAC, ROAS, and channel mix; funnel metrics, including registration → first purchase → repeat purchase; retention metrics, including cohorts and LTV curves; and financial overview.
Support the performance marketing team with ad-hoc SQL analysis and Python scripts for automation.
Design and analyze A/B tests across the funnel, document results, and help drive action based on findings.
Document tracking conventions and data definitions in the knowledge base.


What We Look For


3+ years of experience as a Data Analyst, Web Analyst, or Marketing Analyst working with web subscription or e-commerce products.
Strong web analytics expertise, including funnels, attribution, tracking logic, and marketing performance measurement.
Experience working with performance marketing data.
Experience working with raw and semi-processed data from Facebook Ads, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, or similar platforms, including data validation and cleanup.
Strong understanding of subscription business metrics, including LTV, cohorts, retention, recurring revenue, CAC, and payback period.
Experience setting up web tracking from scratch at least once, for example for a new product, new funnel, or new market.
Advanced SQL: window functions, CTEs, query optimization, and working with complex datasets.
Proficient Python: pandas, NumPy, analysis scripts, and basic automation.
Experience with BI tools such as Tableau, Looker, Power BI, or similar.
Comfortable working with ambiguity and building structure where it does not yet exist.
Open, clear, and proactive communication style.
High level of ownership and initiative.


Nice to have


Hands-on experience with GA4.
Hands-on experience with GTM, including client-side and server-side implementation.
Experience working with PSP data.
A/B testing experience.
Tableau experience: dashboards, calculated fields, parameter actions.
BigQuery experience: large datasets, cost optimization, and performance optimization.
Experience with dbt.


We Offer


Full ownership and freedom to set up tracking from scratch for a new product, with no legacy constraints.
Real business impact: you will work directly with a performance team running live campaigns, not build dashboards no one reads.
A strong data team around you (10+people), including Data Engineers, Data Scientists, BI specialists, and AI specialists to consult with, learn from, and grow with.
Modern stack: SQL, BigQuery, dbt, GA4, GTM, Tableau, and Python.
Openness to new practices: the team has already implemented GrowthBook, dbt, server-side tracking, and GenAI skills for analysts.
Career path: strong performance opens the door to real career growth.
Flexible schedule and remote-friendly setup.
Competitive compensation with regular performance reviews.
Medical insurance.
Learning and development budget.

Title: Business Analyst - Data & Analytics
Description:
Business Analyst – Data & Analytics




We are looking for a proactive Business Analyst with a strong understanding of Data & Analytics to bridge the gap between business and technology. You will work closely with stakeholders to identify, prioritize and shape analytics capabilities, translating business needs into scalable end-to-end analytics solutions.

We are looking for someone who understands the broader analytics landscape, enjoys working closely with the business, and can help design effective data-driven solutions.




In this role, you will:

Partner closely with business stakeholders to gather, analyse and challenge business requirements for analytics solutions.
Translate business needs into clear functional requirements and scalable end-to-end solution designs.
Collaborate with technical teams to deliver analytics and reporting solutions.
Support multiple analytics initiatives by identifying business priorities and helping shape the analytics roadmap.
Coordinate testing, business validation and successful deployment of new analytics capabilities.
Proactively identify opportunities to improve existing analytics solutions and processes.
Act as the key interface between business and technology, building strong stakeholder relationships and driving collaboration.
Stay up to date with modern analytics technologies and best practices.




Requirements

3+ years of experience as a Business Analyst in Data & Analytics, Business Intelligence or a similar domain.
Strong understanding of data, analytics and reporting, with the ability to design end-to-end business solutions.
Ability to translate business requirements into technical specifications and communicate effectively with both business and technical stakeholders.
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills.
Dynamic, hands-on and proactive personality with a strong ownership mindset.
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced environment with multiple parallel initiatives.

Title: Businesss Analyst - SQL , Data Analysis, IFRS, Accounting
Description:
Businesss Analyst - SQL , Data Analysis, IFRS, Accounting - this is a long term contract opportunity for our Zürich based client in the Financial Services sector.

Your tasks:



Drive the business analysis for Finance IT, supporting the implementation of the Finance Target Architecture in Azure Databricks.
Work with business stakeholders to capture, clarify, and translate requirements into actionable work items in Azure DevOps.
Collaborate with software development and Finance IT teams to support delivery of finance data flows and reporting capabilities.
Analyse, document, and maintain the current state of finance and business data, identifying gaps against system requirements.
Define source system data derivation rules and propose practical, workable solutions.
Prepare test data, coordinate testing and UAT, and support go-live and BAU transition activities.



Your experience/knowledge:



Experienced Business Analyst on complex projects, ideally with agile delivery methods.
Strong SQL and data analysis skills, with the ability to work through complex data issues critically.
Experience documenting clear, high-quality requirements and implementation work items in English.
Knowledge of finance and accounting, with the ability to analyse data models and business processes.
Experience supporting testing, UAT coordination, and solution validation in a collaborative environment.
Language skills: English - mother tongue, English - fluent



Your soft skills:



Strong communication skills and the ability to work effectively with diverse stakeholders.
Structured, methodical, and detail-oriented approach to problem-solving and documentation.
Curious, solution-oriented mindset with the confidence to work in a complex international environment.



Location: Zürich, Switzerland

Work setting: Hybrid

Sector: Financial Services

Start: ASAP

Duration: 12 months +

Workload: 100%

Ref.Nr.: BH 24402

Take the next step and send us your resume along with a daytime phone number where we can reach you.

Due to Swiss work permit restrictions, we can only consider applications from Swiss nationals, EU citizens, and current work-permit holders for Switzerland.

We welcome applications from individuals of all genders, age groups, sexual orientations, personal expressions, ethnic backgrounds, and religious beliefs. Therefore, there is no requirement to provide gender information or a photo in your application.

As per client requirements, we need information about your marital status, nationality, date of birth, and a valid Swiss work permit. For applicants with disabilities, we are happy to explore potential solutions with our end client.

This job advertisement was prepared with the assistance of AI technology and has undergone human review and approval.

Every application is reviewed personally by our recruiting team - no AI-based screening is used in our selection process.

Title: Excel Expert - Remote
Description:
Job Description

Job Title: Excel Expert

Job Type: Contract

Location: Remote

Job Summary: In this role, you'll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input. No prior experience in AI is required — your domain knowledge is what matters.

Key Responsibilities


Review, analyze, and enhance complex Excel files relevant to business operations, strategy, and data analysis.
Provide detailed feedback and examples of best practices in Excel for operational and consulting use cases (excluding finance).
Design, document, and share templates or solutions that reflect expert knowledge for AI learning purposes.
Collaborate with the customer’s team to identify key workflows and operational challenges represented in Excel documents.
Evaluate and simulate real-world business problems using advanced Excel features and formulas.
Communicate clearly and effectively in written and verbal formats, ensuring precise and comprehensive documentation.
Contribute to the creation of data analysis case studies and operational scenarios leveraging Excel expertise.


Required Skills And Qualifications


Expert-level proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including advanced functions, pivot tables, data validation, and automation tools.
Proven experience developing Excel solutions for business operations, strategy, or consulting environments.
Strong analytical skills and the ability to solve complex, non-financial business problems using Excel.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a keen eye for detail.
Ability to document processes, create instructional materials, and provide clear, actionable feedback.
Experience with data analysis, scenario modeling, and workflow optimization in Excel.
Self-motivated, proactive, and comfortable working independently in a remote setting.


Preferred Qualifications


Background in business operations, strategy, or management consulting.
Familiarity with AI training or contributing to machine learning datasets in a domain capacity.
Experience developing Excel-based tools or dashboards for operational excellence initiatives.

Job Title: Excel Expert

Job Type: Contract

Location: Remote

Job Summary: In this role, you'll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input. No prior experience in AI is required — your domain knowledge is what matters.

Key Responsibilities

  • Review, analyze, and enhance complex Excel files relevant to business operations, strategy, and data analysis.
  • Provide detailed feedback and examples of best practices in Excel for operational and consulting use cases (excluding finance).
  • Design, document, and share templates or solutions that reflect expert knowledge for AI learning purposes.
  • Collaborate with the customer’s team to identify key workflows and operational challenges represented in Excel documents.
  • Evaluate and simulate real-world business problems using advanced Excel features and formulas.
  • Communicate clearly and effectively in written and verbal formats, ensuring precise and comprehensive documentation.
  • Contribute to the creation of data analysis case studies and operational scenarios leveraging Excel expertise.

Required Skills And Qualifications

  • Expert-level proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including advanced functions, pivot tables, data validation, and automation tools.
  • Proven experience developing Excel solutions for business operations, strategy, or consulting environments.
  • Strong analytical skills and the ability to solve complex, non-financial business problems using Excel.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a keen eye for detail.
  • Ability to document processes, create instructional materials, and provide clear, actionable feedback.
  • Experience with data analysis, scenario modeling, and workflow optimization in Excel.
  • Self-motivated, proactive, and comfortable working independently in a remote setting.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Background in business operations, strategy, or management consulting.
  • Familiarity with AI training or contributing to machine learning datasets in a domain capacity.
  • Experience developing Excel-based tools or dashboards for operational excellence initiatives.

Title: BI Data & Analytics Engineer (m/w/d)
Description:
Über unseren Kunden

Unser Kunde ist ein schweizweit tätiges Unternehmen mit einer modernen, datengetriebenen IT Landschaft. Zur Verstärkung des Data & Analytics Teams wird eine engagierte Persönlichkeit gesucht, welche die Weiterentwicklung der Datenplattform aktiv mitgestaltet und einen wichtigen Beitrag zur Digitalisierung und datenbasierten Entscheidungsfindung leistet.

Ihr Aufgabengebiet


Mitarbeit im Bereitschaftsdienst zur Sicherstellung des Betriebs
Beitrag zur kontinuierlichen Optimierung von Geschäftsprozessen im ERP Umfeld
Entwicklung, Betrieb und kontinuierliche Optimierung von Datenintegrations- und ETL-Lösungen
Modellierung und Weiterentwicklung von Datenstrukturen für Reporting-, Analyse- und Self-Service-BI-Anwendungen
Sicherstellung der Qualität, Stabilität und Nachvollziehbarkeit von Datenflüssen, Schnittstellen und Datenmodellen
Analyse fachlicher Anforderungen sowie Übersetzung in nachhaltige Data- und Analytics-Lösungen
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Fachbereichen und Business Analysten zur Weiterentwicklung datenbasierter Prozesse
Mitarbeit beim Betrieb und Ausbau einer modernen Data & Analytics Plattform auf Basis einer hybriden Microsoft Umgebung
Identifikation und Umsetzung technischer Verbesserungen zur Optimierung der Datenlandschaft


Ihr Profil


Abgeschlossene Ausbildung oder Weiterbildung in Informatik, Wirtschaftsinformatik oder einer vergleichbaren Fachrichtung, alternativ relevante Praxiserfahrung im Data Engineering
Berufserfahrung im Bereich Data Engineering, Business Intelligence, Data Warehousing oder Analytics
Gute Kenntnisse in Datenbanken, Datenmodellierung und Datenmanagement
Erfahrung mit Microsoft-Technologien wie SQL Server, T-SQL, Power BI, SSIS, Tabular Models oder Microsoft Fabric von Vorteil
Fähigkeit, fachliche Anforderungen zu analysieren und in technische Lösungen zu übersetzen
Analytische, strukturierte und selbstständige Arbeitsweise sowie Freude an der Zusammenarbeit mit unterschiedlichen Fachbereichen
Sehr gute Deutsch- sowie gute Englischkenntnisse


Benefits


Moderne Data & Analytics Umgebung mit vielseitigen Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten
Flexible Arbeitsmodelle mit Homeoffice und Gleitzeit
Eigenverantwortliches Arbeiten in einem kollegialen und wertschätzenden Umfeld
Moderne Infrastruktur und zentral gelegener Arbeitsplatz mit guter ÖV-Anbindung
Attraktive Anstellungsbedingungen inklusive 13. Monatslohn
Umfangreiche interne Aus- und Weiterbildungsmöglichkeiten
Vergünstigungen im Bereich Verpflegung, Hotellerie, Freizeit sowie weitere attraktive Mitarbeiterbenefits

Title: Controller & Data Analyst junior (m/f/x)
Description:
Your Responsibilities

As a Junior Controller & Data Analyst (m/f/x), you monitor expenses by cost center,
You analyze and comment on profit and loss variances (financial and analytical),
You support the preparation of budgets by cost center,
You participate in monthly closings as well as in the preparation of future plans,
You develop KPIs and performance BI reports/tools in collaboration with the company’s functional managers,
You carry out specific analyses and drive optimization initiatives,


Your Profil

You hold a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business Administration, Finance, or a related field,
You have at least 2 to 3 years of experience in controlling or data analysis, ideally in an industrial environment,
You are fluent in French and English and have good knowledge of German,
You are proficient in the MS Office suite and have solid knowledge of an ERP system,
You are autonomous, flexible, and able to work effectively within a team,
You stand out through your analytical skills, communication abilities, and your capacity to assert yourself.


Our Offer
Can we inspire you to take on a responsible position in a dynamic and independent family business? We offer you room for ideas, a secure job, modern and family-friendly employment conditions such as flexible working hours, home office, a company restaurant, training opportunities as well as many benefits. The workplace is located in Bulle, surrounded by the wonderful nature of the Gruyère region. Start planning your personal and professional development with us.
In order to ensure a fair application process, we cannot accept applications by post or email.
This position is subject to a background check process.

Have we awoken your interest? Then we look forward to receiving your online application. If you have any questions, please contact Nadege MALAN.
One Passion. Many Opportunities.

The Company
Liebherr Machines Bulle SA is the centre of excellence for combustion engines (diesel and gas engines) and hydraulic components (axial piston pumps and motors). The company develops and produces various products, which find their use in building construction and civil engineering, mining, agriculture, decentralised energy, machine and plant engineering, as well as aerospace.

Location
Liebherr Machines Bulle SA
7, Rue Hans-Liebherr
1630 Bulle
Switzerland (CH)

Contact
Nadege MALAN
026 913 31 11

Title: Analytics & Business Operations Associate
Description:
About Viscadia

Viscadia is a consulting firm whose mission is to empower life sciences companies with clarity and confidence through forecasting. With offices in the United States, Switzerland, and India, we are well-equipped to provide the support that global pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies need to make decisions quickly and with confidence. We are deeply committed to a vision of shared growth and success for our clients, colleagues, and communities.




Role Overview

The Analytics and Business Operations Associate will support the Viscadia's European and global needs across three areas: Global analytics and project delivery, business development and marketing support, and day-to-day Swiss business operations. Primary responsibilities may vary based on business priorities, but are expected to be approximately:

Global analytics & project delivery: ~50%
Business development & marketing support: ~30%
Swiss business operations: ~20%




Based in Lucerne, this is a broad, hands-on role for a professional who combines strong analytical ability with a proactive, well-organized approach. The incumbent will support global life sciences consulting engagements across forecasting and commercial insights while helping build and operate Viscadia's growing European presence. As part of a lean and growing Swiss team, this role suits a proactive, hands-on go-getter who takes ownership, is comfortable wearing multiple hats, and thrives with limited oversight in a fast-moving environment.




This position reports directly to the Associate Principal based in Lucerne, who leads Viscadia's presence in the region.




Key Responsibilities:




Global Analytics & Project Delivery

Work as part of active client engagement teams, supporting the development and maintenance of forecasting and analytics models for client engagements, including data gathering, structuring, and quality checks.
Conduct secondary research and synthesize insights on therapeutic areas, disease states, treatment paradigms, competitive landscapes, and pharmaceutical market dynamics into clear, actionable summaries.
Build and format analyses, charts, and presentations that translate findings into clear, evidence-based narratives for client and internal audiences.
Support engagement managers and senior leadership on project tasks, ensuring accuracy, thoroughness, and on-time delivery.
Collaborate with global delivery teams in the United States and India across time zones to provide seamless, round-the-clock client support.




Business Development & Marketing Support

Research pharmaceutical companies, therapeutic areas, licensing opportunities and strategic accounts to support business development initiatives across Europe.
Support the preparation of proposals, pitch materials, case studies, and capability demonstrations.
Maintain CRM records and track the pipeline of leads, opportunities, and follow-ups.
Support marketing activities, including content, collateral, conference planning and execution, LinkedIn and digital presence, and webinar coordination.
Help organize client meetings, conferences, and regional networking activities.




Operations & Administration

Coordinate and support the day-to-day operations of the Swiss office, ensuring efficient and reliable running.
Support compliance, documentation, and coordination with local authorities and service providers.
Maintain organized records and support scheduling, travel, and general administrative needs.
Liaise with banks, accountants, legal counsel, government authorities, immigration partners and other external service providers.




Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Business, Economics, Life Sciences, Data Analytics or a related quantitative field.
Master’s degree in Engineering, Business, Economics, Life Sciences, Data Analytics or a related quantitative field will be preferred.
1-3 years of relevant experience in consulting, analytics, forecasting, pharma or life sciences, data, or related environments. Internship experience is valued.
Strong analytical and structured problem-solving capabilities.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint.
Working familiarity with Python, SQL, VBA, dashboarding tools, or analytics platforms is preferred.
Exposure to generative AI and productivity tools to improve research, analysis, presentation development and operational efficiency.
Interest in healthcare, pharmaceuticals, forecasting, analytics, or consulting environments.
Excellent organization, attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple priorities independently in a fast-paced environment
Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to explain complex analyses clearly.
Ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced, team-oriented environment while demonstrating initiative and ownership.
Self-starter with a strong work ethic, curiosity, adaptability, and eagerness to learn.
German is advantageous but not required for local business development and administration.
Authorized to work in Switzerland requiring no visa sponsorships.




Employment Terms

Location: Lucerne, Switzerland (On-site with hybrid working arrangement with an expectation of 3 days regular presence in office).
Part-time 75%, with the possibility of increasing to 100% subject to business needs and individual performance.
Occasional travel within Europe to support client meetings, conferences and business development activities.




Why Join Viscadia?

A high-impact, intellectually stimulating environment with direct exposure to senior leadership and global life sciences clients.
Gain exposure to forecasting, commercial strategy, business development, and Swiss business operations.
Develop consulting, analytics, and client-facing skills in a high-growth environment.
Competitive compensation complemented by performance-based recognition that rewards excellence, innovation, and ownership
Global exposure and a collaborative culture, working with cross-functional teams across the United States, Switzerland, and India
Structured opportunities to grow across analytics, commercial, and operations, with a clear pathway to broaden scope and responsibility over time.




Viscadia is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. We encourage applications from all qualified individuals regardless of race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national origin, marital status, disability, or veteran status.

We are a world leading Engine MRO service provider, headquartered in Zurich, Switzerland, with over 90 years of operational experience. Our unwavering dedication to innovation, excellence and environmental responsibility propels us forward on our journey to becoming the leading, most customer-centric, and sustainable Engine focused MRO worldwide.

Working with an extensive network of partners and business development offices in Europe, the USA, Asia and the Middle East, we offer comprehensive, fully customized solutions for the Maintenance, Repair and Overhaul of aircraft engines, airframes and components sales business including impeccable technical support to over 500 customers worldwide. Our mission: We safely power your flight. As an Intern Data Analyst (New Engines Department) you will involve working on digitalization improvement projects and supporting the Operations Team and Management to work more efficiently. Your tasks


Create Power Apps Tool supporting Engine Shop Visit Management
Create Power BI Dashboard supporting Operations Management Report

Strong interest in the Aerospace MRO industry and to work in a regulated environment

Title: Product Analyst (Muslima)
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Product Analyst (Muslima) based in Switzerland.

This role is focused on driving data-informed product decisions across large-scale social discovery platforms used by millions of users worldwide. You will play a key role in analyzing product behavior, identifying trends, and uncovering growth opportunities that directly impact user engagement and business performance. The position combines deep analytical work with product thinking, requiring you to translate raw data into actionable insights and testable hypotheses. You will be responsible for designing and evaluating A/B tests, supporting product experimentation, and contributing to revenue and growth modeling. The environment is highly international, remote-first, and fast-moving, with a strong focus on experimentation, data-driven decision-making, and continuous product improvement. This is an opportunity to work on globally distributed products that aim to improve how people connect and interact online.

Accountabilities


Analyze core product metrics on a regular basis to detect anomalies, trends, and early signals of performance changes.
Design, validate, and evaluate A/B tests, ensuring statistical rigor and reliable interpretation of results.
Support the creation, prioritization, and validation of product hypotheses using data-driven insights.
Explore large datasets to identify growth opportunities and uncover hidden relationships between product metrics.
Calculate and monitor unit economics, including LTV, CAC, and ARPU across user cohorts.
Build forecasting and impact models to estimate revenue and business outcomes of new features and initiatives.
Collaborate closely with product and growth teams to support experimentation and decision-making.


Requirements


Degree in Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, or a related technical field.
Strong understanding of probability theory and mathematical statistics applied to real-world analysis.
Hands-on experience with data analysis tools such as SQL, Python, and Excel.
Proven experience designing and analyzing A/B tests in a product or growth environment.
Solid knowledge of marketing and product metrics, with practical application experience.
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to work independently and proactively identify opportunities.
Ability to extract insights from complex datasets and translate them into clear business recommendations.


Benefits


Fully remote work opportunity in a global, distributed team.
Competitive compensation shared during the recruitment process.
28 days of annual vacation plus 7 wellness days per year.
Financial support for professional training, conferences, and industry events.
Corporate discounts for English language learning.
Health benefits or annual health-related compensation support.
Workplace equipment support or remote setup reimbursement (depending on location).
Referral bonuses for successful candidate recommendations.
Internal reward system with redeemable perks and experiences.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

This is an opportunity to work on globally distributed products that aim to improve how people connect and interact online.

Accountabilities

  • Analyze core product metrics on a regular basis to detect anomalies, trends, and early signals of performance changes.
  • Design, validate, and evaluate A/B tests, ensuring statistical rigor and reliable interpretation of results.
  • Support the creation, prioritization, and validation of product hypotheses using data-driven insights.
  • Explore large datasets to identify growth opportunities and uncover hidden relationships between product metrics.
  • Calculate and monitor unit economics, including LTV, CAC, and ARPU across user cohorts.
  • Build forecasting and impact models to estimate revenue and business outcomes of new features and initiatives.
  • Collaborate closely with product and growth teams to support experimentation and decision-making.

Requirements

  • Degree in Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, or a related technical field.
  • Strong understanding of probability theory and mathematical statistics applied to real-world analysis.
  • Hands-on experience with data analysis tools such as SQL, Python, and Excel.
  • Proven experience designing and analyzing A/B tests in a product or growth environment.
  • Solid knowledge of marketing and product metrics, with practical application experience.
  • Strong analytical mindset with the ability to work independently and proactively identify opportunities.
  • Ability to extract insights from complex datasets and translate them into clear business recommendations.

Benefits

  • Fully remote work opportunity in a global, distributed team.
  • Competitive compensation shared during the recruitment process.
  • 28 days of annual vacation plus 7 wellness days per year.
  • Financial support for professional training, conferences, and industry events.
  • Corporate discounts for English language learning.
  • Health benefits or annual health-related compensation support.
  • Workplace equipment support or remote setup reimbursement (depending on location).
  • Referral bonuses for successful candidate recommendations.
  • Internal reward system with redeemable perks and experiences.

How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: BI & Data Engineer (m/w/d)
Description:
Über unseren Kunden

Unser Kunde ist eine grosse öffentliche Organisation mit einer modernen IT- und Datenlandschaft und treibt die Digitalisierung ihrer Geschäftsprozesse konsequent voran. Zur Verstärkung eines neu aufgebauten Kompetenzteams wird eine engagierte Persönlichkeit gesucht, die moderne Datenplattformen mitgestaltet und datenbasierte Entscheidungen nachhaltig unterstützt.

Ihr Aufgabengebiet


Weiterentwicklung einer modernen Data-Plattform sowie Konzeption skalierbarer und zukunftsfähiger Datenarchitekturen
Integration neuer Datenquellen sowie Entwicklung und Optimierung automatisierter ETL- und Datenverarbeitungsprozesse
Sicherstellung von Datenqualität, Verfügbarkeit und Performance für Reporting- und Analyseanwendungen
Weiterentwicklung von Dashboards, Reportinglösungen und datengetriebenen Auswertungen
Mitarbeit an Digitalisierungs- und Datenprojekten in enger Zusammenarbeit mit Fachbereichen und IT
Umsetzung von Data-Governance- und Datenschutzanforderungen sowie Dokumentation der entwickelten Datenlösungen
Identifikation und Umsetzung von Optimierungspotenzialen innerhalb der Datenplattform und Datenprozesse


Ihr Profil


Abgeschlossenes Studium in Informatik, Wirtschaftsinformatik oder einer vergleichbaren Fachrichtung
Erste Berufserfahrung im Data Engineering, Business Intelligence oder Datenmanagement, alternativ motivierte Berufseinsteiger mit entsprechender Ausbildung
Gute Kenntnisse in SQL, Datenmodellierung und relationalen Datenbanken
Erfahrung oder Interesse an modernen Data-Plattformen, ETL-Prozessen sowie Technologien wie Microsoft Fabric, Power BI oder Python
Analytische, strukturierte und lösungsorientierte Arbeitsweise
Freude an der Zusammenarbeit mit unterschiedlichen Fachbereichen und an der Weiterentwicklung datengetriebener Lösungen
Sehr gute Deutsch- sowie gute Englischkenntnisse


Benefits


Möglichkeit, den Aufbau einer modernen Data-Plattform aktiv mitzugestalten
Spannende Digitalisierungs- und Datenprojekte mit viel Eigenverantwortung
Moderne Arbeitsumgebung mit flexiblen Arbeitsmodellen und Homeoffice-Möglichkeiten
Attraktive Aus- und Weiterbildungsmöglichkeiten
Überdurchschnittliche Sozialleistungen und zeitgemässe Anstellungsbedingungen
Kollegiales Arbeitsumfeld mit kurzen Entscheidungswegen und viel Gestaltungsspielraum
Sinnstiftende Tätigkeit in einem zukunftsorientierten Umfeld mit langfristiger Perspektive

Title: Data Analyst Supply chain & Service client
HTML:
QoQa c'est avant tout près d'un million de membres de notre magnifique Qommunauté qui découvrent chaque jour des offres dénichées par nos équipes. Notre mission: émerveiller au quotidien.


Notre succès, nous le devons au formidable travail de nos plus de 240 loutres qui s'engagent quotidiennement pour proposer des expériences, des découvertes et un service au top.


Aujourd'hui, nous recherchons la perle rare pour rejoindre l'équipe Data!


Ta mission, si tu l'acceptes:

Ton objectif : transformer la donnée brute en levier stratégique afin de soutenir les départements Opérations (supply chain et service client). Tu garantiras la fiabilité des indicateurs clés pour permettre un pilotage proactif et éclairé de notre organisation.


  • Maîtrise complète de la pipeline : Tu pilotes l'intégralité du cycle de vie de la donnée, de la préparation de l'extraction (en collaboration avec les Data Engineers) depuis le DWH jusqu'à la mise en production de dashboards dans notre outil BI;
  • Partenaire Business : En étroite collaboration avec les équipes métiers, tu traduis leurs enjeux opérationnels en solutions analytiques concrètes;
  • Projets : Tu participeras également à différents projets transverses de l'équipe, selon les priorités du moment.


Parlons de toi et de tes compétences:


Ton expérience - Ta formation

  • Bachelor ou Master en informatique, gestion des données, ingénierie ou domaine équivalent;
  • Minimum 7 ans d'expérience confirmée en Data Analysis / BI, incluant une expertise approfondie des flux Supply Chain (stocks, approvisionnements) et du Service Client (KPIs de satisfaction, suivi de la performance des services de support).


Tes compétences - Tes qualités

  • Excellente maîtrise de SQL et des bases de données relationnelles;
  • Expérience confirmée avec DBT (Data Build Tool) et maîtrise de Github pour le versioning;
  • Bonne maîtrise Tableau Software, pour transformer les données en rapports clairs et percutantsM
  • Une connaissance approfondie de l'ERP Odoo (structure des tables sous-jacentes) est un atout majeur;
  • Approche rigoureuse et orientée résultats;
  • Capacité à collaborer avec les métiers, à comprendre leurs besoins et à concevoir des solutions pertinentes.


Le plus : ton appétence pour l'IA et les outils modernes est sans limite!


Pour que tu restes au taQuet, on t'offre:

  • Un environnement sain, où tu peux être toi même;
  • Une ambiance et un cadre de travail agréables dans notre QG;
  • Un temps de travail flexible, et hybride;
  • Un système de gouvernance en Holacratie;
  • Des avantages sociaux aux petits oignons (5 semaines de vacances, LPP avantageuse, congé maternité et paternité au-delà du minimum légal, etc.);
  • Des rabais et contributions financières: plan de mobilité, abonnement téléphone, repas de midi, sport, etc.);
  • La possibilité de formation continue et de développement.


Les prochaines étapes

Tu as ce qu'il faut pour relever ce défi ?


Pour entrer dans la course, envoie nous au minimum un CV et une lettre ou vidéo de motivation personnalisée. Fais parler ta créativité et raconte-nous pourquoi tu serais la personne idéale pour ce job. Quels sont tes super-pouvoirs: Surprends-nous!


Nous te remercions (et la planète aussi) de ne pas envoyer de candidature papier.


Si ton parcours correspond avec nos attentes pour le poste, nous t'inviterons à découvrir notre tanière et à nous faire découvrir tes talents sur le terrain. Dans le cas contraire, tu recevras un e-mail dans les meilleurs délais.


Alors maintenant, jette-toi à l'eau et tente ta chance!


Entrée en fonction : dès que possible (mais on pourra t'attendre si tu es la bonne personne)

Type de contrat : CDI

Taux: 100%

Lieu : Bussigny

Title: Document Reviewer - Remote
Description:
Job Description

Job Title: Document Reviewer

Job Type: Contract

Location: Remote

Job Summary: In this role, you'll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input.

Key Responsibilities


Carefully review large volumes of structured and unstructured data, ensuring accuracy and completeness.
Identify and flag issues related to data quality, consistency, and potential risks involving Personal Identifiable Information (PII).
Annotate, classify, and, where necessary, redact sensitive information to maintain privacy and compliance standards.
Follow strict guidelines, taxonomies, and workflow processes for data categorization and validation.
Perform quality assurance (QA) checks to verify data integrity and validate outputs.
Maintain detailed records of reviewed documents and actions taken to ensure traceability and accountability.
Collaborate with the customer's team, providing clear and concise written and verbal communication about findings and recommendations.


Required Skills And Qualifications


Proven experience in document review, auditing, annotation, redaction, or data classification at scale.
Exceptional attention to detail with a high level of accuracy in repetitive tasks.
Familiarity with handling and protecting Personal Identifiable Information (PII).
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to document findings and communicate effectively with team members.
Demonstrated ability to follow complex guidelines and adhere to established taxonomies.
Experience performing QA or validation work on large data sets.
Ability to balance efficiency with precision in a remote work environment.


Preferred Qualifications


Background in legal, compliance, privacy, auditing, compliance or information security domains.
Prior experience supporting data-driven AI or machine learning projects.
Comfort with collaboration tools and digital workflow management platforms.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Tradition is the interdealer broking arm of Compagnie Financière Tradition and one of the world's largest interdealer brokers in over-the-counter financial and commodity related products. CFT is represented in over 28 countries, employing over 2,500 people.




Tradition’s goal is to provide superior client services. It believes its business success is a direct reflection of its employees and recruits. As such, teamwork, creativity, reliability and integrity are components of a work ethic taken very seriously since the company was founded in 1959.




Tradition is currently seeking to appoint a Data Engineer to be based in our Lausanne office.




Main responsibilities within the Data Engineer position include:




Data Modelling & Business Analysis

Translate business concepts into scalable, well-structured transformation pipelines and analytical models.
Design and build trusted, reusable, and well-documented data products that are discoverable, reliable, and support multiple use cases.
Data product mindset: ability to think in terms of reusable, well-defined, consumer-focused datasets.
Drive data quality, consistency, and modelling best practices.




Monitoring & Platform Reliability

Own and continuously improve the monitoring and observability ecosystem.
Proactively identify issues, performance bottlenecks, and data quality concerns.
Develop alerts, dashboards, and operational procedures that enable rapid issue detection and resolution.




Application Support & Incident Management

Investigate and resolve production incidents across the data platform.
Support a growing user base by responding to operational and business requests.
Collaborate with engineering teams to identify root causes and implement long-term solutions.
Help maintain the reliability and availability of critical data services




Key skills and experience required in this role:

3+ years of experience in Data Engineering, Software Engineering, or a related field.
Proven experience supporting production systems and monitoring data pipelines or applications, as well as building and maintaining analytical data models.
Strong experience with SQL, Python, dbt, Docker, Kubernetes, OpenSearch or similar logging tools, Prometheus and Grafana (or equivalent observability tools), and cloud-native data platforms and modern data architectures.
Ability to translate complex business needs into data models and products, with a strong focus on business value; knowledge of dimensional modeling and analytics engineering is a plus.
Master’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or a related field.
Excellent English communication skills; proactive, collaborative, and self-driven with a strong sense of ownership and accountability.




Tradition do not accept agency CV’s. Please do not forward CV’s to our employees or Talent team. Tradition are not accountable for any fees related to unsolicited resumes. The Talent team will reach out to trusted agents when required.

Please note, due to the large volume of applications for this position, only suitable candidates will be contacted. If you have not heard from us within 14 days, unfortunately, your application has been unsuccessful.

Tradition welcome all suitable applications and are an equal opportunity employer who value diversity. All employment is decided on the basis of qualifications and merit.

By applying for this role, you agree that we may retain your details on our system for a period of 6 months and may contact you for any future vacancies that may arise within the Tradition Group.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Who we are




SII Group is a trusted technology partner, SII provides high value added solutions for the IT projects of many large corporations. Since its founding in 1979, the Company has been providing solutions adapted to its clients’ needs, by relying on :




> Its acknowledged expertise in various industries and sectors

> Proven 'turnkey' solutions

> An efficient quality system

> Adaptable, evolving services




Today, with a staff of more than 18'000, SII Group is supporting companies across 20 countries.




At SII Switzerland we pay attention to the personal and professional wellbeing of our employees. We place our collaborators at the heart of our actions and activities. If you are motivated by the perspective of joining a big, trusted and recognized group, then let's meet !




To develop our consulting offer and support one of our clients, we are looking for a talented Data Engineer.




Missions

Develop & Scale Pipelines: You design and implement robust ETL/ELT pipelines to process large, complex volumes of data (Big Data) using both code-based and visual tools.
Semantic Data Modeling: You translate complex business processes into logical data models and help build a centralized, cross-departmental enterprise data model.
Data Quality & Lineage: You implement automated quality checks and monitor data flows from source to end product to guarantee seamless data lineage.
Code & Transformation: You write clean, high-performance code in PySpark and SQL to clean, aggregate, and prepare data for downstream analysis.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: You work closely with Data Scientists, Business Analysts, and business departments to quickly translate their requirements into production-ready data structures.




Profile

Initial practical experience in Data Engineering (as a Junior, e.g., through relevant internships/working student roles; as a Mid-Level, approx. 1–3 years of professional experience).
Solid knowledge of Python (especially in a Big Data context with PySpark) as well as proficiency in writing complex SQL queries.
Good understanding of modern data architectures (Data Lakehouse, distributed systems) and ideally experience with integrated all-in-one data platforms.
Familiar with relational and non-relational databases. You enjoy bringing order to unstructured mountains of data.
Analytical mindset, work in a structured manner, and possess a strong willingness to learn, allowing you to quickly adapt to new, complex software ecosystems.




For information

Start date : September
Location : Zurich - CH
Duration : permanent contract
Language : fluent in English, German is a plus

Description:

Build Pipelines in Foundry: You develop and scale data pipelines (ETL/ELT) directly within Palantir Foundry (e.g., via Code Repositories, Pipeline Builder) and integrate data from a wide variety of source systems.
Data Modeling & Ontology: You help map our data into the Foundry Ontology, making complex business logic tangible and accessible for analysts and end-users.
Data Quality & Lineage: You implement health checks, monitor data quality across the entire data lineage process, and ensure that pipelines run stably.
Code & Transformation: You write clean, efficient code (primarily in PySpark and SQL) to clean and transform large volumes of data.
Team Collaboration: You work closely with Data Scientists, Analysts, and business departments to translate their requirements into functioning data models within Foundry.




Profile

Initial professional experience in Data Engineering (as a Junior, e.g., through relevant internships/working student roles; as a Mid-Level, approx. 1–3 years in a permanent role).
Good to excellent knowledge of Python (ideally experience with PySpark or Pandas) and are highly proficient in SQL.
Prior experience with Foundry (e.g., Contour, Code Repositories, Ontology) is a massive plus. If not: You have a strong foundation in data processing and are highly motivated to dive deep into the Palantir ecosystem.
Solid basic understanding of relational databases, data architectures, and the challenges of working with distributed systems (Big Data).
Solution-oriented, think analytically, and enjoy diving into complex datasets and learning new tools.




For information

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Data Engineer based in Switzerland.

This role offers the opportunity to build and optimize a modern data platform that powers marketing intelligence, customer insights, and revenue growth across global products. As a Data Engineer, you will design, develop, and maintain scalable data pipelines that transform complex customer data into actionable business value. You will work across the full data lifecycle, from ingestion and transformation to activation and analytics, collaborating with engineering and business teams in a fast-moving technology environment. The position combines hands-on technical challenges with opportunities to influence architecture, data quality, and automation practices. You will use modern cloud technologies and AI-assisted development workflows to create reliable, high-performing data solutions at scale.

Accountabilities


Build and maintain robust data ingestion pipelines from various sources, including REST APIs, SFTP, and database replication systems into cloud data platforms.
Develop and optimize data transformation workflows using dbt, following structured bronze, silver, and gold layer modeling practices.
Design and maintain data models supporting identity resolution, customer segmentation, attribution, and marketing activation use cases.
Build integrations with external marketing platforms and services, enabling data synchronization, campaign delivery, and feedback collection.
Develop and manage AWS Lambda functions, Airflow workflows, and orchestration processes to ensure reliable data operations.
Implement data quality checks, monitoring, alerting, and validation frameworks to maintain accuracy and trust in business-critical datasets.
Manage cloud infrastructure and data platform components using infrastructure-as-code practices with Terraform.
Investigate and resolve data incidents, including pipeline failures, quality issues, and sensitive data exposure risks.
Contribute to continuous improvement of engineering practices, automation, documentation, and scalable data architecture.


Requirements


4+ years of professional experience designing, developing, and deploying production-grade data pipelines using Python.
Strong expertise in SQL development, including complex queries, optimization techniques, and performance tuning in cloud data environments.
Hands-on experience with Snowflake, including data loading, external stages, security controls, warehouses, and optimization practices.
Strong experience with dbt Core for data transformation, testing, incremental models, and analytics engineering workflows.
Solid understanding of data warehouse design principles, including dimensional modeling, star and snowflake schemas, facts, dimensions, and slowly changing dimensions.
Experience designing conceptual, logical, and physical data models.
Proven experience building and operating ETL/ELT pipelines using Airflow or similar orchestration tools.
Familiarity with modern AI-assisted development workflows and daily use of AI coding tools for development, debugging, and code review.
Knowledge of AWS services such as S3, Lambda, IAM, and EventBridge, along with Terraform and CI/CD practices.
Experience with Git, Docker, data quality frameworks, and data lineage practices is highly valued.
Strong problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and ability to work effectively in a remote, collaborative engineering environment.
Experience with streaming technologies, marketing platform integrations, or iGaming-related products is considered a plus.


Benefits


Opportunity to work on scalable, high-impact products within a growing technology environment.
Fully remote working opportunity across eligible locations.
Flexible working environment designed to support autonomy and work-life balance.
Competitive salary package with performance-based incentives.
Exposure to modern cloud technologies, data architecture patterns, and AI-powered engineering workflows.
Collaborative and supportive team culture with opportunities for professional growth and technical development.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Engineer (H/F)
Description:
Chez 𝗖𝗕𝗧𝗪 (Collaboration Betters The World), entreprise mondiale de conseil en technologie, la collaboration est la pierre angulaire de l’innovation et du succès, et le fondement de tout ce que nous mettons en œuvre.

Notre mission : transformer les entreprises et les communautés pour un monde meilleur.

Présents dans plus de 20 pays et 35 villes, nous allions une expertise mondiale avec une compréhension locale des enjeux de nos clients.




Au sein de la Service Line "Data Analytics & IA", nous développons une approche qui vise à accompagner nos clients dans la définition de leurs stratégies en matière de Data Science et d’IA, d’Analytics et Business Intelligence, de Data et plateformes. L’objectif est de les accompagner à chaque étape de leurs parcours afin de les aider à libérer le potentiel de leurs données.




Que vas-tu faire ?




Concrètement au sein de l'expertise Data Engineering, tu seras en charge de :




1. Conception & Architecture

Concevoir des architectures Data scalables et sécurisées : Data Lake, Data Warehouse.
Réaliser des estimations de coûts et comparer objectivement différentes technologies et architectures.
Participer aux choix technologiques en fonction des enjeux métiers et contraintes techniques.
Définir et promouvoir les bonnes pratiques d’ingénierie et d’architecture Data.




2. Développement & Industrialisation

Construire et industrialiser des pipelines batch et temps réel.
Implémenter des pratiques CI/CD, observabilité, gouvernance et data quality.







Quel est ton profil ?




Tu as une expérience significative de plus de 7 ans en data engineering.
Tu es issu d’une formation dans le domaine de la Data niveau Bac +5/Ecole d'ingénieur
Tu détiens une expertise sur une Data Platform moderne idéalement Data Bricks ou Microsoft Fabric
Tu maîtrises un cloud public (Azure, AWS ou GCP).
Tu as de très bonnes compétences en SQL et Python.
Tu disposes de solides connaissances en architecture Data moderne.
Tu as une forte aisance relationnelle notamment pour l'animation d'atelier variés (conception, ateliers agiles, retros et cadrages).




Notre processus de recrutement :

1ère étape : Rencontre avec un membre de l’équipe RH pour introduire le poste, l’environnement de travail et comprendre tes motivations ;

2ème étape : Rencontre avec les équipes de la Service Line pour faire connaissance mais aussi te challenger techniquement ;

3ème étape : Rencontre avec un membre de la direction et l’un de nos sales pour te challenger sur ta compréhension de CBTW, le partage de valeurs communes et ta motivation pour nous rejoindre.




Ce que nous te proposons :

Collaborer au sein d'une entreprise internationale dynamique et ouverte d'esprit où tes idées sont entendues et valorisées ;
Accéder à des perspectives d’évolution professionnelle tout au long de ta carrière via des formations et des certifications ;
Bénéficier d'un suivi de carrière sur mesure avec ton référent RH et des points réguliers avec ton Business Manager lors de tes missions ;
Intégrer des communautés te permettant de partager des centres d'intérêts communs ou expertises techniques ;
Créer du lien avec des collaborateurs passionnés à travers différents évènements internes (afterworks, soirées annuelles, séminaires, …).




Nous avons hâte d'échanger avec toi !

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Data Engineer (Modern Data Platforms)

Start Date: July/August 2026
Duration: 24 months
Workload: 100%


Main Responsibilities:
Design, develop, and maintain end-to-end data pipelines (ETL/ELT) using Spark, SQL, and related technologies.
Implement business logic, data governance, security, and retention requirements within pipelines.
Translate business and technical requirements into scalable data solutions and detailed designs.
Collaborate with business stakeholders, product owners, and technical teams to address data needs and issues.
Contribute to data architecture, ensuring scalable and high-performance system design.
Support continuous delivery through effective governance, transparency, and communication.
Stay up to date with emerging data technologies and support proof-of-concept initiatives.
Required Background:
5+ years of experience working with modern data technologies.
Strong experience building data ingestion pipelines for data warehouse, data lake, or lakehouse architectures.
Hands-on expertise with big data and open-source tools (e.g., Spark, Hadoop, Hive, Kafka, dbt, ELK), ideally in a Cloudera environment.
Proficiency in Python and strong SQL skills.
Solid understanding of data modelling, scalable system design, and data engineering best practices.
Experience in financial services (e.g., trade lifecycle, risk management).
Familiarity with lakehouse technologies such as Apache Iceberg.
Experience with relational and NoSQL databases (e.g., Postgres, SQL Server, Cassandra).
Exposure to workflow orchestration (Airflow, NiFi), streaming (Kafka, Spark Streaming), and CI/CD pipelines.
Knowledge of Agile methodologies and testing practices.
Experience with cloud platforms (Databricks, Snowflake, Azure Synapse) - nice-to-have
Understanding of microservices and APIs (REST/SOAP)  - nice-to-have
Exposure to .NET/C#  - nice-to-have
You bring strong communication skills 



We believe in equal opportunity for all and actively encourage applications from diverse backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives.
Source Group International Ltd is acting as an Employment Business in relation to this vacancy

Title: Data Engineer - 884308/1
Description:
We are looking for a Data Engineer for our client in Zürich, Switzerland.




Key Information:

Project start: Immediately
Project duration: 6 months with option for extension
Location: Zürich
Workload: 80-100%, 1-2 Days Homeoffice










Responsibilities







Design, develop, and maintain data pipelines for processing production and test data.
Analyze, validate, and ensure the quality of product and process data to meet technical specifications.
Process large volumes of data generated from industrial production environments and testing systems.
Integrate, transform, and prepare structured and unstructured data sources for analysis and downstream applications.
Develop and optimize ETL processes for automated data processing and integration.
Implement Data Engineering and Data Science solutions across the entire data lifecycle.
Collaborate with production, engineering, and cross-functional teams to identify data-driven optimization opportunities.
Develop Python-based applications, tools, and scripts for data analysis, automation, and process improvement.
Ensure software quality through version control, documentation, and adherence to software development best practices.
Support the further development and optimization of the existing data architecture and big data environment.
Utilize and enhance workflow orchestration and data pipeline solutions (e.g., Apache Airflow, Apache NiFi).
Analyze complex datasets from machines, manufacturing systems, and test equipment to generate actionable and reliable insights.










Required Skills







Several years of hands-on experience in Data Engineering, Data Science, or Software Development.
Bachelor's or Master's degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a comparable technical discipline.
Strong Python programming skills with a focus on data processing, automation, and data pipeline development.
Experience designing, implementing, and maintaining ETL processes and data integration solutions.
Proficient in Git and modern software version control systems.
Experience working with large-scale datasets and complex data environments.
Professional experience in an industrial setting, ideally within manufacturing, mechanical engineering, or electrical engineering.
Fluent English skills, both written and spoken; German language skills would be considered a strong advantage.
