Title: Business Analyst / Consultant - Artificial Intelligence & Data - (AI & Insights)
Description:
Job Description

Location: Zurich, Geneva

Work Pattern Percentage: 80-100%

Help our clients navigate the future with confidence and embrace decision making with clear data-driven choices

Deloitte AI & Data helps clients unlock the power of AI and data to deliver better human-centric solutions that catalyze growth and efficiencies. We enable them to leverage data, analytics, modern platforms, and data science & AI technologies to uncover hidden relationships from vast troves of data, generate insights, and inform their decision-making.

We help clients transform their business by designing and implementing data solutions to excel in their markets. Our role is to understand their business problems and use data-driven thinking and technology to achieve outcomes that drive their business forward.

This role is open to candidates with either an AI/Analytics or a Data Engineering/Data Platform background and preference. During the interview process, we will determine the best fit for you within one of these specialized subteams.

We also welcome applications from trainee candidates with limited work experience, provided they are available for full-time work (not returning to school). After 4–6 months in a trainee role, we will reassess readiness for a Business Analyst role.

For this role, we are currently offering start dates from February 2026.

How You Can Make An Impact


Design and implement AI and data strategies and modern solutions – including data management, platforms and AI use cases – that enable clients to achieve their business objectives across regulatory, cost and revenue pillars.
Provide architectural, technical and organizational thought leadership to guide complex data and AI transformations with significant organizational implications.
Drive Data & AI use cases from business value concept to production.
Build and maintain trusted client relationships through project delivery and business development, expanding your network internally and externally.
Support business development opportunities to generate new projects.
Be a role model for people you work with by demonstrating strong work ethic, leadership, and subject matter competence.
Continuously grow your knowledge across industries and share insights with Deloitte talent.



Who We Are Looking For

Drive and Motivation:


Passionate individuals eager to build a career in AI & Data Consulting.
Strong drive to learn, master new topics and manage multiple activities independently in a fast-paced environment.



Consulting and Communication Skills:


Strong writing and Microsoft Power Point skills: Ability to design and shape meaningful presentations.
Strong Conceptual Capability: Ability to consolidate diverse requirements into coherent solution designs, simplifying complex problems.
Business-IT Translator: Ability to act as the bridge between business needs and technical solutions.
Attention to detail and ability to communicate technical specifics clearly in both written and oral forms.



Technical expertise:


First practical experience with AI & analytics solutions is highly recommended.
Familiarity with SQL and Python is a plus.
Experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure or GCP, including working with Large Language Models (LLMs) and related services is a plus.
Knowledge of data platforms tools (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks, Dataiku) and data visualisation tools (e.g., PowerBI, Tableau) is a plus.
Strong engineering or mathematics background is advantageous.



Language Proficiency:


Fluent in English; fluency in German and/or French is highly preferred to facilitate communication with diverse teams and stakeholders.



Industry Experience:


First relevant experience in consulting, tech companies, or industry-specific roles, particularly in AI & data-driven fields.
Previous experience in the Banking, Insurance, Life Science or Healthcare sector is advantageous.



Your team

Become part of a diverse and multinational team of 50+ highly motivated individuals located in our Zurich or Geneva offices. We specialise in AI & data – how to structure it, how to source it, how to transform it, how to store it, and how to consume it. Join our fast-paced and fun team, working alongside highly talented colleagues on varied and exciting projects, with access to excellent and continuous learning opportunities.

Deloitte AI & Data is the leading data & analytics consulting team in Switzerland. You will have a chance to work with outstanding colleagues and have access to variety of clients, vendors, and technologies. Our business is growing fast and there are plenty market opportunities to develop career in the direction that you want.

We will help you grow both personally and professionally: through our trainings, mentoring programmes, and on-the-job learning, but also by working with clients and vendors across different industries.

Life at Deloitte

At Deloitte, your ideas create impact and spark meaningful change for our clients, people and society. Your unique expertise, background and perspective helps us find new ways into the most complex challenges so we can brighten the futures of those our work affects. At Deloitte, you can build a career that inspires and energises you. At Deloitte, you can choose your impact.

In Switzerland, we provide industry-specific services in the areas of Audit & Assurance, Tax & Legal, Strategy, Risk & Transactions Advisory, and Technology & Transformation. With close to 3,000 employees at six locations, we serve companies and organisations of all sizes in all industry sectors. We offer meaningful and challenging work, an inclusive and supportive environment where you can be your best self and where you will never stop growing. You will benefit from flexible and hybrid working conditions, wellbeing and parental programmes as well as many opportunities to connect, collaborate and learn.

Join us and become part of a global network of like-minded people dedicated to making a difference, whether by addressing climate change or improving 100 million futures by 2030. Want to know more about opportunities at Deloitte? Visit www.apply.deloitte.ch .

How To Apply

We look forward to hearing from you! Please click on “apply now” to submit a complete application, including CV, cover letter, references and degree certificates.

If you have any questions, please contact Aarabi at talent@deloitte.ch


We consider all qualified applicants for employment regardless of race, ethnicity, religious beliefs, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age or disability, in accordance with applicable law. Research shows that women are less likely to apply for roles unless they match all the criteria; don’t hold yourself back – apply today.



© 2026. For information, contact Deloitte AG. All rights reserved. Deloitte AG is an equal opportunities employer.

Description:
Data Analytics Engineer - Python, Cloudera vacancy for our Basel based client in the Banking sector.

Your tasks:




Develop code and implement end-to-end data pipelines with optimal extraction, transformation and loading of data from diverse sources using Spark, SQL and other modern technologies.
Collaborate with business analysts and stakeholders to identify, capture, and analyse requirements, translating them into effective technical solutions.
Contribute to the design and evolution of the overall data architecture, ensuring alignment with modern data management practices.
Maintain and support data pipelines on shared data platforms, while incorporating business rules, security and retention requirements.
Support proof of concepts, stay current with industry standards and assist with the adoption of new technologies in the data platform area.







Your experience/knowledge:




Minimum 5 years of hands-on experience working with modern data technologies, including building data ingestion pipelines for data warehouses or lake/lakehouse architectures.
Proven experience with Python and open-source data technologies such as Hadoop, Spark, Hive, Kafka, or similar, preferably with Cloudera.
Strong background in data modelling, design patterns, and building scalable applications.
Experience within banking/financial institutions, with knowledge of financial instruments, trade lifecycle, or risk management is an advantage.
Language skills: English







Your soft skills:




Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to explain complex technical concepts in simple terms.
Collaborative mindset and capacity to work effectively within multidisciplinary teams.
Strong problem-solving skills and a commitment to continuous learning and improvement.







Location: Basel, Switzerland

Work setting: Hybrid

Sector: Banking

Start: ASAP

Duration: 24 months

Workload: 100%

Title: Intern Data Science & Machine Learning (80-100%)
Description:
Electricity flows with us. On behalf of Switzerland and in cooperation with international partners, we do our utmost every day to develop the best solutions for the Swiss electricity grid.

Our team in Aarau is looking for an

Intern Data Science & Machine Learning (80-100%) - 1583

Power Switzerland's Energy Future – From Day One

At Swissgrid, we don't just observe the energy transition, we engineer it. As the operator of Switzerland's national transmission grid, we sit at the nexus of a rapidly evolving European electricity market. Our Market Optimization & Decision Support team develops forecasting and automation solutions that directly support grid operators in their 24/7 mission to ensure a reliable power supply. By joining us, you will contribute to impactful work at the heart of Switzerland’s electricity system.

The Internship: Where Learning Meets Real Impact

You’ll work on challenging and meaningful data science and machine learning projects. You’ll gain unique insights into the Swiss transmission system while working alongside a motivated, international team of data scientists and engineers. The work is real from day one—real data, real decisions, and real impact. You’ll have access to a modern, cloud-native tech stack and the latest AI development tools, and you’ll be encouraged to bring and run with your creative, AI-driven approaches to complex data problems.

What You Will Work On

Roll up your sleeves for challenges like these:


Build and ship forecasting models that solve real energy-sector problems with a sharp focus on safety, scalability, and production robustness. You'll experiment freely: tuning hyperparameters, benchmarking architectures, and proposing novel approaches.
Design and build data pipelines that process large-scale time-series data reliably and efficiently, forming the backbone of model training and deployment workflows.
Explore and analyze data surface hidden patterns, engineer high-signal features, and push forecasting accuracy further.
Work AI-natively – integrate AI tools into your daily development practice to sharpen analysis, accelerate iteration, smoothen workflows and improve efficiencies.
Collaborate and communicate across teams, gather feedback from stakeholders, and make sure your work connects meaningfully to the broader business mission.


What Makes You a Great Fit

We are looking for a builder – someone with the curiosity to experiment and the discipline to deliver. You'll thrive here if you bring:


Academic foundation: Currently pursuing (or recently completed) a Bachelor's or Master's in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Economics, or a related field.
Strong Python skills and solid software engineering instincts – version control, clean code, and reproducible workflows are second nature to you.
Machine learning depth: A genuine understanding of classical ML, statistical methods, time-series modeling, and predictive systems. Hands-on experience with libraries like Scikit-learn, PyTorch, or MLflow is a plus; familiarity with Apache Spark is a bonus.
AI-native mindset: You use GenAI tools to build things, not just to ask questions. Show us what you've already shipped.
Energy domain curiosity: Knowledge of transmission grid operations or electricity markets is a plus – but your drive to learn matters more.
Above all, we value curiosity, agency, and a growth mindset. If you're the kind of person who gets energized by hard problems and wants your work to matter, we'd love to hear from you. 🚀


What We Offer


Unique industry access – an immersive, behind-the-scenes look at Switzerland's electricity infrastructure and the challenges of building the grid of the future.
Meaningful work – varied and exciting tasks within a company unique to Switzerland., with real ownership from the start.
Flexibility – flexible working hours as part of a motivated, welcoming team.
25 days of holiday, plus company holidays between Christmas and New Year.
A modern workplace in the immediate vicinity of Aarau, with a state-of-the-art technical environment.

  • Design and build data pipelines that process large-scale time-series data reliably and efficiently, forming the backbone of model training and deployment workflows.
  • Explore and analyze data surface hidden patterns, engineer high-signal features, and push forecasting accuracy further.
  • Work AI-natively – integrate AI tools into your daily development practice to sharpen analysis, accelerate iteration, smoothen workflows and improve efficiencies.
  • Collaborate and communicate across teams, gather feedback from stakeholders, and make sure your work connects meaningfully to the broader business mission.

What Makes You a Great Fit

We are looking for a builder – someone with the curiosity to experiment and the discipline to deliver. You'll thrive here if you bring:

  • Academic foundation: Currently pursuing (or recently completed) a Bachelor's or Master's in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Economics, or a related field.
  • Strong Python skills and solid software engineering instincts – version control, clean code, and reproducible workflows are second nature to you.
  • Machine learning depth: A genuine understanding of classical ML, statistical methods, time-series modeling, and predictive systems. Hands-on experience with libraries like Scikit-learn, PyTorch, or MLflow is a plus; familiarity with Apache Spark is a bonus.
  • AI-native mindset: You use GenAI tools to build things, not just to ask questions. Show us what you've already shipped.
  • Energy domain curiosity: Knowledge of transmission grid operations or electricity markets is a plus – but your drive to learn matters more.
  • Above all, we value curiosity, agency, and a growth mindset. If you're the kind of person who gets energized by hard problems and wants your work to matter, we'd love to hear from you. 🚀

What We Offer

  • Unique industry access – an immersive, behind-the-scenes look at Switzerland's electricity infrastructure and the challenges of building the grid of the future.
  • Meaningful work – varied and exciting tasks within a company unique to Switzerland., with real ownership from the start.
  • Flexibility – flexible working hours as part of a motivated, welcoming team.
  • 25 days of holiday, plus company holidays between Christmas and New Year.
  • A modern workplace in the immediate vicinity of Aarau, with a state-of-the-art technical environment.

Title: Intern Business Performance & Insights (100%)
Description:
To support our Performance Management & Insights Department at our headquarters in Schaffhausen we are looking for an

Intern Business Performance & Insights (100%)

The primary focus of this internship is to accelerate our data & analytics transformation, enabling management to leverage data for enhanced decision-making and business outcomes. You will play a key role in supporting the development and deployment of performance reports using the Google tech stack, with a strong focus on executive-level reporting and high-quality visualizations.

YOUR MISSION AT IWC.

Performance Report Delivery


Support the delivery of executive (ExCo) performance reports, using Google Looker
Ensure alignment of reports with business KPIs and management priorities
Translate business requirements into clear reporting logic and data structures


Insights & Visualizations


Design clear, compelling, and executive-ready visualizations in Google Looker
Translate complex data into simple, actionable insights for senior stakeholders
Continuously improve usability, storytelling, and layout of reports
Share best practices and contribute to a data-driven culture across the organization


Data Engineering


Integrate and structure diverse data sources to enable consistent and reliable reporting
Ensure data quality and availability for business-critical reporting


How You Become Part Of The Journey.


University degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Finance, or a comparable field
A strong interest in data, analytics, and how they can be applied to solve business problems
Strong proficiency in SQL and experience with data warehousing concepts and technologies
Hands-on personality with a strong business acumen and excellent communication and collaboration skills
First practical experience is required: Familiarity in project-based, analytical settings a plus, e.g., tech, consulting
Business fluent English skills are required, German is a plus


What You Can Expect From Us.

IWC offers you a challenging working environment: rooted in Switzerland, but operating successfully internationally. We combine tradition with innovation and attach great importance to change management, training and further education as well as personal development. Find below a selection of fringe benefits provided by us:


Daily food & beverage allowance of CHF 7, redeem-able in the in-house restaurant and selected restaurants in Schaffhausen.
Yearly health insurance contribution of CHF 2'340 for full-time contracts.
25 days vacation annually (30 days as of the age of 50), 5 additional bridge compensation days.
Special conditions on selected IWC products.
Diverse health & wellbeing initiatives and programs
IWC Sport Club (est. 1972) for sport enthusiasts.
Attractive workplace adjacent to the river rhine, inviting for social activities amongst colleagues.
Memorable internal events (Christmas, summer after work drinks, etc.).
And many more!

Title: Business Analyst / Consultant - Artificial Intelligence & Data - (AI & Insights)
Description:
Job Description

Location: Zurich, Geneva

Work Pattern Percentage: 80-100%

Help our clients navigate the future with confidence and embrace decision making with clear data-driven choices

Deloitte AI & Data helps clients unlock the power of AI and data to deliver better human-centric solutions that catalyze growth and efficiencies. We enable them to leverage data, analytics, modern platforms, and data science & AI technologies to uncover hidden relationships from vast troves of data, generate insights, and inform their decision-making.

We help clients transform their business by designing and implementing data solutions to excel in their markets. Our role is to understand their business problems and use data-driven thinking and technology to achieve outcomes that drive their business forward.

This role is open to candidates with either an AI/Analytics or a Data Engineering/Data Platform background and preference. During the interview process, we will determine the best fit for you within one of these specialized subteams.

We also welcome applications from trainee candidates with limited work experience, provided they are available for full-time work (not returning to school). After 4–6 months in a trainee role, we will reassess readiness for a Business Analyst role.

For this role, we are currently offering start dates from February 2026.

How You Can Make An Impact


Design and implement AI and data strategies and modern solutions – including data management, platforms and AI use cases – that enable clients to achieve their business objectives across regulatory, cost and revenue pillars.
Provide architectural, technical and organizational thought leadership to guide complex data and AI transformations with significant organizational implications.
Drive Data & AI use cases from business value concept to production.
Build and maintain trusted client relationships through project delivery and business development, expanding your network internally and externally.
Support business development opportunities to generate new projects.
Be a role model for people you work with by demonstrating strong work ethic, leadership, and subject matter competence.
Continuously grow your knowledge across industries and share insights with Deloitte talent.



Who We Are Looking For

Drive and Motivation:


Passionate individuals eager to build a career in AI & Data Consulting.
Strong drive to learn, master new topics and manage multiple activities independently in a fast-paced environment.



Consulting and Communication Skills:


Strong writing and Microsoft Power Point skills: Ability to design and shape meaningful presentations.
Strong Conceptual Capability: Ability to consolidate diverse requirements into coherent solution designs, simplifying complex problems.
Business-IT Translator: Ability to act as the bridge between business needs and technical solutions.
Attention to detail and ability to communicate technical specifics clearly in both written and oral forms.



Technical expertise:


First practical experience with AI & analytics solutions is highly recommended.
Familiarity with SQL and Python is a plus.
Experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure or GCP, including working with Large Language Models (LLMs) and related services is a plus.
Knowledge of data platforms tools (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks, Dataiku) and data visualisation tools (e.g., PowerBI, Tableau) is a plus.
Strong engineering or mathematics background is advantageous.



Language Proficiency:


Fluent in English; fluency in German and/or French is highly preferred to facilitate communication with diverse teams and stakeholders.



Industry Experience:


First relevant experience in consulting, tech companies, or industry-specific roles, particularly in AI & data-driven fields.
Previous experience in the Banking, Insurance, Life Science or Healthcare sector is advantageous.



Your team

Become part of a diverse and multinational team of 50+ highly motivated individuals located in our Zurich or Geneva offices. We specialise in AI & data – how to structure it, how to source it, how to transform it, how to store it, and how to consume it. Join our fast-paced and fun team, working alongside highly talented colleagues on varied and exciting projects, with access to excellent and continuous learning opportunities.

Deloitte AI & Data is the leading data & analytics consulting team in Switzerland. You will have a chance to work with outstanding colleagues and have access to variety of clients, vendors, and technologies. Our business is growing fast and there are plenty market opportunities to develop career in the direction that you want.

We will help you grow both personally and professionally: through our trainings, mentoring programmes, and on-the-job learning, but also by working with clients and vendors across different industries.

Life at Deloitte

At Deloitte, your ideas create impact and spark meaningful change for our clients, people and society. Your unique expertise, background and perspective helps us find new ways into the most complex challenges so we can brighten the futures of those our work affects. At Deloitte, you can build a career that inspires and energises you. At Deloitte, you can choose your impact.

In Switzerland, we provide industry-specific services in the areas of Audit & Assurance, Tax & Legal, Strategy, Risk & Transactions Advisory, and Technology & Transformation. With close to 3,000 employees at six locations, we serve companies and organisations of all sizes in all industry sectors. We offer meaningful and challenging work, an inclusive and supportive environment where you can be your best self and where you will never stop growing. You will benefit from flexible and hybrid working conditions, wellbeing and parental programmes as well as many opportunities to connect, collaborate and learn.

Join us and become part of a global network of like-minded people dedicated to making a difference, whether by addressing climate change or improving 100 million futures by 2030. Want to know more about opportunities at Deloitte? Visit www.apply.deloitte.ch .

How To Apply

We look forward to hearing from you! Please click on “apply now” to submit a complete application, including CV, cover letter, references and degree certificates.

If you have any questions, please contact Aarabi at talent@deloitte.ch


We consider all qualified applicants for employment regardless of race, ethnicity, religious beliefs, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age or disability, in accordance with applicable law. Research shows that women are less likely to apply for roles unless they match all the criteria; don’t hold yourself back – apply today.



© 2026. For information, contact Deloitte AG. All rights reserved. Deloitte AG is an equal opportunities employer.

Title: Data Analyst (Onsite) Materialfluss (M/W/D)*
Description:
Die TGW Logistics Group ist weltweit führend in der Entwicklung und Implementierung hochdynamischer, automatisierter und schlüsselfertiger Logistiklösungen. Mit über 27 Standorten und mehr als 4.500 Mitarbeitenden bieten wir massgeschneiderte Logistiklösungen für Unternehmen aus verschiedenen Branchen.

Möchtest du aktiv zur Verbesserung der Anlagenverfügbarkeit und -performance bei Bell Schweiz AG beitragen? Als Onsite Specialist Engineer IT Materialfluss bist du ein zentraler Bestandteil unseres On-Site-Teams. Du überwachst unsere Systeme kontinuierlich, erkennst Optimierungspotenziale und stellst sicher, dass unsere hochmodernen Logistiklösungen jederzeit effizient und störungsfrei laufen. Gemeinsam mit unserem Team setzt du innovative Lösungen um und gewährleistest unseren Kunden einen erstklassigen Service. Werde Teil unserer innovativen Technologie-Community und helfe uns, die Zukunft der Logistik zu gestalten!

Was man bei uns bewegt:


Spezialist und Ansprechpartner bei Anlagenstörungen für alle materialflussbezogene Fragen
Durchführung regelmässiger Systemarbeiten in Bezug auf Software sowie Instandhaltungsarbeiten an der IT-Infrastruktur
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit dem Lagerleitstand, IT und Steuerungs-Support, 2nd Level Support, Entwicklungsabteilung und dem Kunden
Analyse des Kundensystems für Korrekturmassnahmen und Anpassungen sowie Erstellung von Fehleranalysen
Eigenverantwortliches Beheben von Störungen bzw. Eskalationssteuerung an den Customer Support


Was uns überzeugt:


Abgeschlossene Informatik-Ausbildung oder vergleichbare Berufserfahrung
Erfahrung in Programmierung mit SQL, vorzugsweise mit PL/SQL
Fundierte IT-Kenntnisse in aktuellen Technologien (Linux, Windows, Netzwerke, Remote-Access-Tools)
Kommunikations- und Teamfähigkeit sowie eigenständige und strukturierte Arbeitsweise
Bereitschaft zum Schichtdienst und kundenorientierte Arbeitsweise
Kreative und eigenständige Problemlösungskompetenz, sowie Freude an Optimierungen


Was wir bieten:


Wertschätzendes Arbeitsumfeld, motivierte Teams und eine offene Feedbackkultur
Abwechslungsreiche Aufgabengebiete sowie individueller Gestaltungsspielraum
Übernahme von Verantwortung in einem erfolgreichen internationalen Unternehmen mit starkem Fokus auf Kultur und Werte
Sicherer und stabiler Arbeitgeber, der entsprechend der Philosophie „Mensch im Mittelpunkt – lernen und wachsen“ agiert
Grosses Angebot zur fachlichen und persönlichen Weiterentwicklung in Form von Präsenztrainings sowie eLearnings


*TGW ist ein Arbeitgeber, der die Chancengleichheit fördert. Unsere Ausschreibung richtet sich an alle Interessierten.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Title : Data Engineer in Testing

Location : Zurich, Switzerland

Duration: 6-12 months extendable

Work model : 3 days onsite/week

Rate : CHF 680/day

Exp level : 7+ years

Mandatory skills : PySpark, Python, Test Automation(Cucumber / TestNG), Azure

Desired skills : Databricks, Airflow, ALM, Xray or Squash, DevOps, Agile

JD below

Data Engineer in Testing (Minimum – 7 Years work exp – Zurich Location)

Handson experience in working with test automation of complex data transformation pipelines
Experience in Designing and optimizing data testing frameworks
Proven experience in performing functional testing to assess for accuracy, suitability, and functionality.
Ability to create test strategies and plans for manual and automated test methods.
Experienced in Python and pyspark, working knowledge on databricks. Keen on overall processing / compute cost optimization
Experienced with Microsoft Azure and other services, and Airflow
Prior working knowledge of any test management tool ALM, Xray or Squash
Experience with the overall STLC (Software Testing Lifecycle) stages

MUST HAVE SKILLS

Python and pyspark are the language used across multiple agile pod within the team
Knowledge of different types of testing and respective test phases
Test Automation Framework: Cucumber / TestNG as per project setup
Automation Frameworks: Data-Driven Frameworks as per project setup
Build Tools: Gitlab as per project setup
API Testing: Postman / SoapUI as per project setup
Big Data Testing: Python and Pyspark, general usage of spark data processing engine
Understanding of cloud platform (Azure) and DevOps pipelines
Experience in Regulatory and Compliance Domain
Experience with Agile/Scrum methodologies

OTHER BAU QA SKILLS (Supporting Skills)

Experience with test data identification and creation
Have a good understanding of non-functional test concepts (e.g., scalability, performance, and security)
Experience in leveraging Gitlab
Ability to work analytically and systematically throughout the development and testing life cycle
Experience of Agile and iterative development methodologies
Well-developed business communication skills – both written and verbal
Strong personal prioritization and time management
Methodology knowhow on how to ensure that project deliverables meet industry best practices
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and synthesizing skills (should be a quick learner and know how to figure things out with very minimal external help)

Title: Data Product Analyst
Description:
Who is Sonar?

Sonar is driving the future of agent-centric software development. As the leader in AI code review and verification, we solve a critical problem: ensuring that software generated by AI-assisted developers or autonomous agents is reliable, secure, and maintainable.

Integrating seamlessly with Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, Gemini, and Devin, we help over 75% of the Fortune 100 build trusted, reliable, compliant software. Customers who use Sonar are 44% less likely to report an outage due to AI-generated code.

We believe code verification is the critical missing link in the Agent-Centric Development Cycle (AC/DC). Industry giants like Nvidia, ServiceNow, Booking.com, Goldman Sachs, AstraZeneca, and Ford Motor Company count on us to provide independent, explainable, consistent review and governance of their AI-generated code via products like:


SonarQube: The world’s leading AI code review and verification platform.
SonarQube Foundation Agent: Currently topping the leaderboards for agentic software repair.
SonarSweep & Sonar Context Augmentation: Providing the enterprise-grade context and constraints agents need to be truly effective.


Our team operates across global hubs in Austin, Bochum, Dubai, Geneva, London, Singapore, Tokyo, and Washington D.C. We move with a mindset we call CODE:


Committed to our customers and community.
Obsessed with quality.
Deliberate in our decisions.
Effective as one team.


With over $400M in revenue and profitable, fast-paced growth, we are building the backbone of the AI software revolution. If you’re hungry to have an impact, want to build at a fast pace, and ready to work at the forefront of AI, we want to hear from you.

Position Description

We want to make the best decisions possible for our products and to do that, we need to make data-driven decisions. To achieve this goal, we are looking for a talented Data Product Analyst (DPA) to join the product data team.

As a Data Product Analyst (DPA) at Sonar, you are the architect of clarity and the bridge between technical infrastructure and business value. We treat Data as a Product, and you own its entire lifecycle—from dbt SQL modeling and event-based schema governance to high-level executive storytelling.

You will thrive in this role if you enjoy a "full-stack" analytical challenge. You are a proactive project leader who owns the data relationship for your domain. Depending on the need, your impact ranges from empowering users through data literacy to leading complex, end-to-end analyses from initial conception to the final executive summary.

What You Will Do

We believe in using the best tools to stay effective and efficient. You will be expected to master and evolve our data ecosystem:

Data project ownership & delivery


Managing a data project from start to finish using agile method, helping stakeholders to refine their questions into structured data projects, advising stakeholders on what to measure, how to model it, and which tool to use and facilitating stakeholder discussions to bridge the gap between high-level business goals and technical execution.
Deliver end-to-end solutions, from ingestion and modeling to executive-ready visualizations and summaries.


Technical stack


Modeling & Architecture: Build and maintain scalable models using dbt (SQL) and provide critical reviews of event schemas for our event-based platform.
Modern Workflow: Leverage Cursor and Git to accelerate development and ensure code quality and version control.
The Analytics Hub: Own the user experience across Mixpanel, Tableau, Metabase, and Atlan, ensuring they serve as the single source of truth.


Data literacy


Onboarding and empowering users to self-serve, effectively reducing the data team as a bottleneck.
Handling ad-hoc requests ranging from querying specific datasets to providing technical guidance on our tooling landscape.
Participating in Level 1 technical support, including monitoring dbt jobs and Tableau extracts to ensure the reliability of our products.
Project Governance: Use Jira for roadmap visibility and dbt/Confluence/Atlan for high-quality documentation that survives handoffs.


Continuous Platform Improvement

You act as a bridge to Data Engineering. You’ll proactively surface data debt, documentation gaps, and tooling friction to ensure the platform gets simpler and stronger over time. You’ll also ensure high standards for PII handling and security policies are built into every delivery.

Experience And Qualifications


Proven Experience: 3–5+ years in a Data Analyst, Data Product Analyst, or Analytics Engineering role, ideally within a fast-paced environment.
Project Leadership: A track record of taking the lead with stakeholders—successfully translating high-level business ambiguity into structured, actionable data projects. You have experience helping and mentoring other team members.
Technical Mastery: hands-on experience with dbt / SQL (modeling, testing, and documentation).
Hands-on experience with data visualization tools (Tableau, Mixpanel, or similar)
The Modern Workflow: Comfort working in a version-controlled environment (Git) and an interest in leveraging AI-assisted development tools (like Cursor) to drive efficiency.
Data Storytelling: The ability to distill complex findings into executive-ready summaries and visualizations that drive decision-making.
You are fluent in English both written and spoken
Familiarity with data governance practices (PII handling and access control).
Experience in Product Analytics or working directly with Product Managers a nice to have
Familiarity with data engineering concepts (Data Lake, Data Warehouse, ETL / ELT pipeline) and tools (AWS, redshift, Lamdas, Databricks) is a plus


We value diversity, equity, and inclusion

At Sonar, we believe that our diversity is our strength. We are a global company that values and respects different backgrounds, perspectives, and cultures. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive work environment where everyone feels valued and empowered to contribute their best. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and welcome all qualified applicants, regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

If you need any accommodation, please reach out to us at [email protected] .

All offers of employment at Sonar are contingent upon the results of a comprehensive background check and reference verification conducted before the start date.

We do not currently support visa candidates in the US.

Applications that are submitted through agencies or third party recruiters will not be considered.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: GLP Auditor
Description:
Job Title: GLP Auditor

Location: Basel

Contract: Permanent or Temporary




About us

Adryan is an internationally operating services group with offices in The Netherlands and Switzerland. We specialize in providing a full range of services for the life sciences industry, focusing on Project Management, Technical Operations, CQV, and Compliance. Our teams work with top clients in the life sciences sector, ensuring the highest quality and commitment.




Position Summary

We are seeking a quality-oriented GLP Auditor / Data Reviewer (m/f/d). In this role, you will be responsible for reviewing and verifying scientific raw data and paper-based study documentation in accordance with Good Laboratory Practice (GLP).




This role serves as a central interface between the operational laboratory and study departments and Quality Assurance. You will ensure that all data is documented in a complete, consistent, traceable, and regulatory-compliant manner before it is incorporated into reports and regulatory submissions.




Main Responsibilities

Reviewing paper-based study documentation, raw data, laboratory records, and related documents for completeness, consistency, and GLP compliance
Reconciling data from internal databases with the corresponding original documents (paper records)
Identifying, documenting, and tracking deviations, discrepancies, or missing information
Conducting data reviews in accordance with internal QA processes and GLP requirements
Assisting with internal audits and inspections
Creating and maintaining audit trail documentation, review reports, and quality records
Working closely with study directors, scientists, and operational teams to resolve data-related issues
Ensuring data integrity




Requirements:

Experience on Swiss Ordinance on Good Laboratory Practice of 18 May 2005 and the relevant OECD GLP guidelines
QC/QA experience is also considered
Demonstrated proficiency in GLP audit procedures and documentation.
Knowledge of routine analytical methodologies, with an emphasis on chromatography
Knowledge of computerized system validation
Good interpersonal skills with demonstrable tact and diplomacy
Good computer skills including Microsoft Word and Excel. Knowledge of MS-Access and SQL would be a plus
With your openness and understanding of mutuality, your active communication and continuous value assessment you foster and encourage our team mentality. You communicate and work effectively with scientific staff at all levels within the Testing Facility and you see yourself as a creative problem solver.
Good oral and written communication skills in German and English

Title: Analytics & Business Operations Associate
Description:
About Viscadia

Viscadia is a consulting firm whose mission is to empower life sciences companies with clarity and confidence through forecasting. With offices in the United States, Switzerland, and India, we are well-equipped to provide the support that global pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies need to make decisions quickly and with confidence. We are deeply committed to a vision of shared growth and success for our clients, colleagues, and communities.




Role Overview

The Analytics and Business Operations Associate will support the Viscadia's European and global needs across three areas: Global analytics and project delivery, business development and marketing support, and day-to-day Swiss business operations. Primary responsibilities may vary based on business priorities, but are expected to be approximately:

Global analytics & project delivery: ~50%
Business development & marketing support: ~30%
Swiss business operations: ~20%




Based in Lucerne, this is a broad, hands-on role for a professional who combines strong analytical ability with a proactive, well-organized approach. The incumbent will support global life sciences consulting engagements across forecasting and commercial insights while helping build and operate Viscadia's growing European presence. As part of a lean and growing Swiss team, this role suits a proactive, hands-on go-getter who takes ownership, is comfortable wearing multiple hats, and thrives with limited oversight in a fast-moving environment.




This position reports directly to the Associate Principal based in Lucerne, who leads Viscadia's presence in the region.




Key Responsibilities:




Global Analytics & Project Delivery

Work as part of active client engagement teams, supporting the development and maintenance of forecasting and analytics models for client engagements, including data gathering, structuring, and quality checks.
Conduct secondary research and synthesize insights on therapeutic areas, disease states, treatment paradigms, competitive landscapes, and pharmaceutical market dynamics into clear, actionable summaries.
Build and format analyses, charts, and presentations that translate findings into clear, evidence-based narratives for client and internal audiences.
Support engagement managers and senior leadership on project tasks, ensuring accuracy, thoroughness, and on-time delivery.
Collaborate with global delivery teams in the United States and India across time zones to provide seamless, round-the-clock client support.




Business Development & Marketing Support

Research pharmaceutical companies, therapeutic areas, licensing opportunities and strategic accounts to support business development initiatives across Europe.
Support the preparation of proposals, pitch materials, case studies, and capability demonstrations.
Maintain CRM records and track the pipeline of leads, opportunities, and follow-ups.
Support marketing activities, including content, collateral, conference planning and execution, LinkedIn and digital presence, and webinar coordination.
Help organize client meetings, conferences, and regional networking activities.




Operations & Administration

Coordinate and support the day-to-day operations of the Swiss office, ensuring efficient and reliable running.
Support compliance, documentation, and coordination with local authorities and service providers.
Maintain organized records and support scheduling, travel, and general administrative needs.
Liaise with banks, accountants, legal counsel, government authorities, immigration partners and other external service providers.




Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Business, Economics, Life Sciences, Data Analytics or a related quantitative field.
Master’s degree in Engineering, Business, Economics, Life Sciences, Data Analytics or a related quantitative field will be preferred.
1-3 years of relevant experience in consulting, analytics, forecasting, pharma or life sciences, data, or related environments. Internship experience is valued.
Strong analytical and structured problem-solving capabilities.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint.
Working familiarity with Python, SQL, VBA, dashboarding tools, or analytics platforms is preferred.
Exposure to generative AI and productivity tools to improve research, analysis, presentation development and operational efficiency.
Interest in healthcare, pharmaceuticals, forecasting, analytics, or consulting environments.
Excellent organization, attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple priorities independently in a fast-paced environment
Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to explain complex analyses clearly.
Ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced, team-oriented environment while demonstrating initiative and ownership.
Self-starter with a strong work ethic, curiosity, adaptability, and eagerness to learn.
German is advantageous but not required for local business development and administration.
Authorized to work in Switzerland requiring no visa sponsorships.




Employment Terms

Location: Lucerne, Switzerland (On-site with hybrid working arrangement with an expectation of 3 days regular presence in office).
Part-time 75%, with the possibility of increasing to 100% subject to business needs and individual performance.
Occasional travel within Europe to support client meetings, conferences and business development activities.




Why Join Viscadia?

A high-impact, intellectually stimulating environment with direct exposure to senior leadership and global life sciences clients.
Gain exposure to forecasting, commercial strategy, business development, and Swiss business operations.
Develop consulting, analytics, and client-facing skills in a high-growth environment.
Competitive compensation complemented by performance-based recognition that rewards excellence, innovation, and ownership
Global exposure and a collaborative culture, working with cross-functional teams across the United States, Switzerland, and India
Structured opportunities to grow across analytics, commercial, and operations, with a clear pathway to broaden scope and responsibility over time.




Viscadia is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion in the workplace. We encourage applications from all qualified individuals regardless of race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, national origin, marital status, disability, or veteran status.

Title: Business Analyst / Consultant - Artificial Intelligence & Data - (AI & Insights)
Description:
Job Description

Location: Zurich, Geneva

Work Pattern Percentage: 80-100%

Help our clients navigate the future with confidence and embrace decision making with clear data-driven choices

Deloitte AI & Data helps clients unlock the power of AI and data to deliver better human-centric solutions that catalyze growth and efficiencies. We enable them to leverage data, analytics, modern platforms, and data science & AI technologies to uncover hidden relationships from vast troves of data, generate insights, and inform their decision-making.

We help clients transform their business by designing and implementing data solutions to excel in their markets. Our role is to understand their business problems and use data-driven thinking and technology to achieve outcomes that drive their business forward.

This role is open to candidates with either an AI/Analytics or a Data Engineering/Data Platform background and preference. During the interview process, we will determine the best fit for you within one of these specialized subteams.

We also welcome applications from trainee candidates with limited work experience, provided they are available for full-time work (not returning to school). After 4–6 months in a trainee role, we will reassess readiness for a Business Analyst role.

For this role, we are currently offering start dates from February 2026.

How You Can Make An Impact


Design and implement AI and data strategies and modern solutions – including data management, platforms and AI use cases – that enable clients to achieve their business objectives across regulatory, cost and revenue pillars.
Provide architectural, technical and organizational thought leadership to guide complex data and AI transformations with significant organizational implications.
Drive Data & AI use cases from business value concept to production.
Build and maintain trusted client relationships through project delivery and business development, expanding your network internally and externally.
Support business development opportunities to generate new projects.
Be a role model for people you work with by demonstrating strong work ethic, leadership, and subject matter competence.
Continuously grow your knowledge across industries and share insights with Deloitte talent.



Who We Are Looking For

Drive and Motivation:


Passionate individuals eager to build a career in AI & Data Consulting.
Strong drive to learn, master new topics and manage multiple activities independently in a fast-paced environment.



Consulting and Communication Skills:


Strong writing and Microsoft Power Point skills: Ability to design and shape meaningful presentations.
Strong Conceptual Capability: Ability to consolidate diverse requirements into coherent solution designs, simplifying complex problems.
Business-IT Translator: Ability to act as the bridge between business needs and technical solutions.
Attention to detail and ability to communicate technical specifics clearly in both written and oral forms.



Technical expertise:


First practical experience with AI & analytics solutions is highly recommended.
Familiarity with SQL and Python is a plus.
Experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure or GCP, including working with Large Language Models (LLMs) and related services is a plus.
Knowledge of data platforms tools (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks, Dataiku) and data visualisation tools (e.g., PowerBI, Tableau) is a plus.
Strong engineering or mathematics background is advantageous.



Language Proficiency:


Fluent in English; fluency in German and/or French is highly preferred to facilitate communication with diverse teams and stakeholders.



Industry Experience:


First relevant experience in consulting, tech companies, or industry-specific roles, particularly in AI & data-driven fields.
Previous experience in the Banking, Insurance, Life Science or Healthcare sector is advantageous.



Your team

Become part of a diverse and multinational team of 50+ highly motivated individuals located in our Zurich or Geneva offices. We specialise in AI & data – how to structure it, how to source it, how to transform it, how to store it, and how to consume it. Join our fast-paced and fun team, working alongside highly talented colleagues on varied and exciting projects, with access to excellent and continuous learning opportunities.

Deloitte AI & Data is the leading data & analytics consulting team in Switzerland. You will have a chance to work with outstanding colleagues and have access to variety of clients, vendors, and technologies. Our business is growing fast and there are plenty market opportunities to develop career in the direction that you want.

We will help you grow both personally and professionally: through our trainings, mentoring programmes, and on-the-job learning, but also by working with clients and vendors across different industries.

Life at Deloitte

At Deloitte, your ideas create impact and spark meaningful change for our clients, people and society. Your unique expertise, background and perspective helps us find new ways into the most complex challenges so we can brighten the futures of those our work affects. At Deloitte, you can build a career that inspires and energises you. At Deloitte, you can choose your impact.

In Switzerland, we provide industry-specific services in the areas of Audit & Assurance, Tax & Legal, Strategy, Risk & Transactions Advisory, and Technology & Transformation. With close to 3,000 employees at six locations, we serve companies and organisations of all sizes in all industry sectors. We offer meaningful and challenging work, an inclusive and supportive environment where you can be your best self and where you will never stop growing. You will benefit from flexible and hybrid working conditions, wellbeing and parental programmes as well as many opportunities to connect, collaborate and learn.

Join us and become part of a global network of like-minded people dedicated to making a difference, whether by addressing climate change or improving 100 million futures by 2030. Want to know more about opportunities at Deloitte? Visit www.apply.deloitte.ch .

How To Apply

We look forward to hearing from you! Please click on “apply now” to submit a complete application, including CV, cover letter, references and degree certificates.

If you have any questions, please contact Aarabi at talent@deloitte.ch


We consider all qualified applicants for employment regardless of race, ethnicity, religious beliefs, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age or disability, in accordance with applicable law. Research shows that women are less likely to apply for roles unless they match all the criteria; don’t hold yourself back – apply today.



© 2026. For information, contact Deloitte AG. All rights reserved. Deloitte AG is an equal opportunities employer.

Job Description

Location: Zurich, Geneva

Work Pattern Percentage: 80-100%

Help our clients navigate the future with confidence and embrace decision making with clear data-driven choices

Deloitte AI & Data helps clients unlock the power of AI and data to deliver better human-centric solutions that catalyze growth and efficiencies. We enable them to leverage data, analytics, modern platforms, and data science & AI technologies to uncover hidden relationships from vast troves of data, generate insights, and inform their decision-making.

We help clients transform their business by designing and implementing data solutions to excel in their markets. Our role is to understand their business problems and use data-driven thinking and technology to achieve outcomes that drive their business forward.

This role is open to candidates with either an AI/Analytics or a Data Engineering/Data Platform background and preference. During the interview process, we will determine the best fit for you within one of these specialized subteams.

We also welcome applications from trainee candidates with limited work experience, provided they are available for full-time work (not returning to school). After 4–6 months in a trainee role, we will reassess readiness for a Business Analyst role.

For this role, we are currently offering start dates from February 2026.

How You Can Make An Impact

  • Design and implement AI and data strategies and modern solutions – including data management, platforms and AI use cases – that enable clients to achieve their business objectives across regulatory, cost and revenue pillars.
  • Provide architectural, technical and organizational thought leadership to guide complex data and AI transformations with significant organizational implications.
  • Drive Data & AI use cases from business value concept to production.
  • Build and maintain trusted client relationships through project delivery and business development, expanding your network internally and externally.
  • Support business development opportunities to generate new projects.
  • Be a role model for people you work with by demonstrating strong work ethic, leadership, and subject matter competence.
  • Continuously grow your knowledge across industries and share insights with Deloitte talent.


Who We Are Looking For

Drive and Motivation:

  • Passionate individuals eager to build a career in AI & Data Consulting.
  • Strong drive to learn, master new topics and manage multiple activities independently in a fast-paced environment.


Consulting and Communication Skills:

  • Strong writing and Microsoft Power Point skills: Ability to design and shape meaningful presentations.
  • Strong Conceptual Capability: Ability to consolidate diverse requirements into coherent solution designs, simplifying complex problems.
  • Business-IT Translator: Ability to act as the bridge between business needs and technical solutions.
  • Attention to detail and ability to communicate technical specifics clearly in both written and oral forms.


Technical expertise:

  • First practical experience with AI & analytics solutions is highly recommended.
  • Familiarity with SQL and Python is a plus.
  • Experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure or GCP, including working with Large Language Models (LLMs) and related services is a plus.
  • Knowledge of data platforms tools (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks, Dataiku) and data visualisation tools (e.g., PowerBI, Tableau) is a plus.
  • Strong engineering or mathematics background is advantageous.


Language Proficiency:

  • Fluent in English; fluency in German and/or French is highly preferred to facilitate communication with diverse teams and stakeholders.


Industry Experience:

  • First relevant experience in consulting, tech companies, or industry-specific roles, particularly in AI & data-driven fields.
  • Previous experience in the Banking, Insurance, Life Science or Healthcare sector is advantageous.


Your team

Become part of a diverse and multinational team of 50+ highly motivated individuals located in our Zurich or Geneva offices. We specialise in AI & data – how to structure it, how to source it, how to transform it, how to store it, and how to consume it. Join our fast-paced and fun team, working alongside highly talented colleagues on varied and exciting projects, with access to excellent and continuous learning opportunities.

Deloitte AI & Data is the leading data & analytics consulting team in Switzerland. You will have a chance to work with outstanding colleagues and have access to variety of clients, vendors, and technologies. Our business is growing fast and there are plenty market opportunities to develop career in the direction that you want.

We will help you grow both personally and professionally: through our trainings, mentoring programmes, and on-the-job learning, but also by working with clients and vendors across different industries.

Life at Deloitte

At Deloitte, your ideas create impact and spark meaningful change for our clients, people and society. Your unique expertise, background and perspective helps us find new ways into the most complex challenges so we can brighten the futures of those our work affects. At Deloitte, you can build a career that inspires and energises you. At Deloitte, you can choose your impact.

In Switzerland, we provide industry-specific services in the areas of Audit & Assurance, Tax & Legal, Strategy, Risk & Transactions Advisory, and Technology & Transformation. With close to 3,000 employees at six locations, we serve companies and organisations of all sizes in all industry sectors. We offer meaningful and challenging work, an inclusive and supportive environment where you can be your best self and where you will never stop growing. You will benefit from flexible and hybrid working conditions, wellbeing and parental programmes as well as many opportunities to connect, collaborate and learn.

Join us and become part of a global network of like-minded people dedicated to making a difference, whether by addressing climate change or improving 100 million futures by 2030. Want to know more about opportunities at Deloitte? Visit www.apply.deloitte.ch .

How To Apply

We look forward to hearing from you! Please click on “apply now” to submit a complete application, including CV, cover letter, references and degree certificates.

If you have any questions, please contact Aarabi at talent@deloitte.ch

  • We consider all qualified applicants for employment regardless of race, ethnicity, religious beliefs, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age or disability, in accordance with applicable law. Research shows that women are less likely to apply for roles unless they match all the criteria; don’t hold yourself back – apply today.


© 2026. For information, contact Deloitte AG. All rights reserved. Deloitte AG is an equal opportunities employer.

Title: Platform Analyst
Description:
The Good Guys Company (TGGC) is a boutique consultancy for the buy-side in EMEA. We work with asset and wealth managers on operating model transformation, AI strategy, and special situations. We are small, senior, and quick. We are hiring a Platform Analyst: someone who has run part of an investment platform from the inside and wants to leverage that experience to work across many firms rather than one.




ABOUT TGGC

We sit where strategy meets the operating reality of asset and wealth managers: how portfolios are run, how trades get done, how operations hold together, and how AI changes all three. We are deliberately small and deliberately senior. There is no pyramid to climb and no layer between you and the people making the decisions, whether ours or the clients’.




THE ROLE

As a Platform Analyst, you bring real buy-side operating experience into our client work. You have sat on the other side of the table, in portfolio management, trading, or operations, and you know how an investment platform actually works, where it breaks, and what it takes to fix it. You will use that to help asset and wealth managers rethink how they operate.




In this role you will:

Diagnose how a client's platform works today, and where it loses time, money, or control
Design target operating models across the front, middle, and back office
Turn strategy into changes people understand and can implement
Bring an investor's lens to questions of cost, scale, and risk
Work directly with client leadership and alongside TGGC's senior team, with little in between




WHAT YOU BRING

5+ years on the buy-side, in portfolio management, trading, operations/technology. We weight them equally.
You have been operating / analyzing / advising / project managing as an internal. We are not interested in experience from pure external consulting.
Industry clout: You have a good feeling for how investment platforms run, and an instinct for what makes them better.
Curiosity that does not switch off. You ask why, you dig, you want to understand the whole system.
Clear communication. You can make something complex land with a portfolio manager and a COO in the same room.
A commercial mindset. You see where value sits and how decisions move it.
The right to work in Switzerland.
English is essential. German and/or French is a plus.




HOW WE WORK

Senior from day one. You work with experienced people on real problems, with exposure to decision-makers of leading players in the asset management industry from the start.
Quick. Decisions get made fast. When something needs doing, you do it.
Room to grow. Take on as much as you can carry, as fast as you can carry it. The ceiling is yours to find.
Flexible. Work where you work best. We care that work gets done, not where you sit, as long as the client does not need you on site.
Real impact. A small firm, high caliber clients, real problems, your fingerprints on the outcome.




HOW TO APPLY

Send a note to info@thegoodguyscompany.ch and share your thoughts with us on these three things (one page in total):

1.  A project you are most proud of: what it achieved, and what your role in it was.

2.  The Good Asset Management AG manages CHF 20bn in private and public markets strategies with a cost/income ratio of 92%. You are assigned to this manager. What do you do next?

3.  How managing funds for investors will look in the year 2040.




We read every one.

Title: Product Analyst (Muslima)
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Product Analyst (Muslima) based in Switzerland.

This role is focused on driving data-informed product decisions across large-scale social discovery platforms used by millions of users worldwide. You will play a key role in analyzing product behavior, identifying trends, and uncovering growth opportunities that directly impact user engagement and business performance. The position combines deep analytical work with product thinking, requiring you to translate raw data into actionable insights and testable hypotheses. You will be responsible for designing and evaluating A/B tests, supporting product experimentation, and contributing to revenue and growth modeling. The environment is highly international, remote-first, and fast-moving, with a strong focus on experimentation, data-driven decision-making, and continuous product improvement. This is an opportunity to work on globally distributed products that aim to improve how people connect and interact online.

Accountabilities


Analyze core product metrics on a regular basis to detect anomalies, trends, and early signals of performance changes.
Design, validate, and evaluate A/B tests, ensuring statistical rigor and reliable interpretation of results.
Support the creation, prioritization, and validation of product hypotheses using data-driven insights.
Explore large datasets to identify growth opportunities and uncover hidden relationships between product metrics.
Calculate and monitor unit economics, including LTV, CAC, and ARPU across user cohorts.
Build forecasting and impact models to estimate revenue and business outcomes of new features and initiatives.
Collaborate closely with product and growth teams to support experimentation and decision-making.


Requirements


Degree in Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, or a related technical field.
Strong understanding of probability theory and mathematical statistics applied to real-world analysis.
Hands-on experience with data analysis tools such as SQL, Python, and Excel.
Proven experience designing and analyzing A/B tests in a product or growth environment.
Solid knowledge of marketing and product metrics, with practical application experience.
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to work independently and proactively identify opportunities.
Ability to extract insights from complex datasets and translate them into clear business recommendations.


Benefits


Fully remote work opportunity in a global, distributed team.
Competitive compensation shared during the recruitment process.
28 days of annual vacation plus 7 wellness days per year.
Financial support for professional training, conferences, and industry events.
Corporate discounts for English language learning.
Health benefits or annual health-related compensation support.
Workplace equipment support or remote setup reimbursement (depending on location).
Referral bonuses for successful candidate recommendations.
Internal reward system with redeemable perks and experiences.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: System Analyst
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a System Analyst based in Switzerland.

As a System Analyst, you will play a key role in shaping and enhancing a modern cross-border payment infrastructure used by global businesses. Working at the intersection of product, engineering, and business teams, you will translate complex business needs into scalable technical solutions. This position offers the opportunity to work on payment systems, banking integrations, APIs, and cloud-based architectures while contributing to a rapidly evolving fintech environment. You will help improve platform performance, streamline processes, and support the delivery of innovative financial products. If you enjoy solving complex technical challenges and collaborating across multidisciplinary teams, this role offers significant impact and long-term growth opportunities.

Accountabilities


Analyze, document, and continuously improve payment infrastructure, acquiring platforms, and banking integrations.
Translate business requirements into detailed system requirements, technical specifications, workflows, and solution designs.
Collaborate closely with Product, Engineering, and business stakeholders to ensure technical solutions align with strategic objectives.
Support the integration of new payment providers, banking partners, and third-party services.
Define API requirements, data flows, system workflows, integration logic, and acceptance criteria for new and existing initiatives.
Review technical implementation details, including API contracts, data models, logs, and technical documentation where appropriate.
Identify opportunities to improve scalability, automation, operational efficiency, and system reliability.
Produce and maintain comprehensive documentation, including functional specifications, process diagrams, user stories, and technical notes.
Validate delivered solutions, clarify requirements throughout development, and contribute to successful product delivery.


Requirements


At least 3 years of experience as a System Analyst, Business Systems Analyst, Solution Analyst, or in a similar role, ideally within fintech, banking, payments, or financial services.
Strong experience documenting business processes, system integrations, APIs, technical workflows, and solution designs.
Solid understanding of payment processing, card acquiring, banking integrations, or financial infrastructure.
Experience collaborating closely with software engineering teams in agile product environments.
Good knowledge of cloud-based architectures, with exposure to AWS services such as Lambda, DynamoDB, Aurora DB, SQS, EventBridge, or similar technologies considered an advantage.
Ability to analyze APIs, technical documentation, system behavior, and data flows, with basic understanding of code logic being a plus.
Proficiency with documentation and modeling tools such as Jira, Confluence, BPMN, UML, or sequence diagrams.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
English proficiency at B2 level or higher.
Proactive mindset, strong ownership, and experience leveraging AI tools to improve analysis, documentation, research, or problem-solving.


Benefits


Flexible working hours with hybrid and remote work options.
Private health insurance and wellness benefits.
Competitive paid time off, including 12 additional personal leave days each year and a day off on your birthday.
Apple hardware and modern productivity tools.
Opportunity to work on complex payment infrastructure and large-scale financial technology projects.
Collaborative and ambitious environment with strong opportunities for professional development and career growth.
Direct involvement in building scalable banking and payment solutions with meaningful business impact.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). Title: Un/Una capoprogetto per la revisione della Classificazione Svizzera degli Interventi Chirurgici CHOP
Description:
Neuchâtel, Suisse (e lavoro a domicilio) | 80-100%

Il contributo che può dare


Pianificare e realizzare la revisione della Classificazione Svizzera degli Interventi Chirurgici CHOP
Pianificare e realizzare la revisione della CHOP, compresa la gestione del progetto
Gestire attivamente gli stakeholder e i gruppi di interesse nonché la comunicazioneesterna
Garantire la tracciabilità del lavoro (redazione di verbali e documentazione)
Assicurare la rappresentanza nel programma DigiSanté e nei comitati associati
Partecipare a progetti di sviluppo in relazione alle classificazioni nazionali e internazionali nonché al programma DigiSanté


Che cosa La contraddistingue


Diploma universitario in scienze dell'informazione con perfezionamento in campo medico o diploma universitario in ambito sanitario (medico, infermiere/a FH, terapista o formazione equivalente) con formazione continua in scienze dell'informazione (biblioteconomia o documentazione) (SUP o titolare di una formazione equivalente) o formazione di base come documentalista medico/a. In alternativa, un diploma universitario in informatica medica
Conoscenze e interesse per linguaggi d'indicizzazione documentale medica e sistemi di terminologia (classificazioni, nomenclature, terminologie, tesauri ecc.)
Buone competenze comunicative (scritte e orali)
Esperienza nella gestione di progetti


In sintesi

Sarà responsabile della pianificazione e della realizzazione della revisione della CHOP con il coinvolgimento degli stakeholder interessati del settore sanitario e farà parte del team del programma DigiSanté per la trasformazione digitale nel settore sanitario.

Che cosa offriamo


Lavorare per la Svizzera Ci adoperiamo per il modello di successo svizzero e lavoriamo per il benessere della popolazione.
Vivere la diversità La concretizzazione delle pari opportunità ci consente di esprimere al meglio le nostre competenze e di contemplare punti di vista differenti.
In forma sul posto di lavoro Forniamo supporto e consulenza alle nostre collaboratrici e ai nostri collaboratori sulla tematica della salute fisica e mentale.


Tutti i benefit

La statistica conta. Anche per voi.

L'Ufficio federale di statistica (UST) è il centro di competenza nazionale della statistica pubblica e il fulcro dell'ecosistema dei dati svizzero. Produce informazioni statistiche, si impegna, attraverso la gestione dei dati a livello nazionale, a favore di dati armonizzati e utilizza metodi di scienza dei dati per estrarre ulteriori informazioni dai dati. In questo modo l'UST contribuisce a formare l'opinione pubblica fornendo un importante contributo alla moderna governance democratica.

Informazioni complementari

Domande sul posto di lavoro

Markus Trutmann Responsabile di settore +41 58 46 30630

Domande sulla candidatura

Marianne Zumbach Responsabile presso il Servizio del personale

  • Partecipare a progetti di sviluppo in relazione alle classificazioni nazionali e internazionali nonché al programma DigiSanté

Che cosa La contraddistingue

  • Diploma universitario in scienze dell'informazione con perfezionamento in campo medico o diploma universitario in ambito sanitario (medico, infermiere/a FH, terapista o formazione equivalente) con formazione continua in scienze dell'informazione (biblioteconomia o documentazione) (SUP o titolare di una formazione equivalente) o formazione di base come documentalista medico/a. In alternativa, un diploma universitario in informatica medica
  • Conoscenze e interesse per linguaggi d'indicizzazione documentale medica e sistemi di terminologia (classificazioni, nomenclature, terminologie, tesauri ecc.)
  • Buone competenze comunicative (scritte e orali)
  • Esperienza nella gestione di progetti

In sintesi

Sarà responsabile della pianificazione e della realizzazione della revisione della CHOP con il coinvolgimento degli stakeholder interessati del settore sanitario e farà parte del team del programma DigiSanté per la trasformazione digitale nel settore sanitario.

Che cosa offriamo

  • Lavorare per la Svizzera Ci adoperiamo per il modello di successo svizzero e lavoriamo per il benessere della popolazione.
  • Vivere la diversità La concretizzazione delle pari opportunità ci consente di esprimere al meglio le nostre competenze e di contemplare punti di vista differenti.
  • In forma sul posto di lavoro Forniamo supporto e consulenza alle nostre collaboratrici e ai nostri collaboratori sulla tematica della salute fisica e mentale.

Tutti i benefit

La statistica conta. Anche per voi.

L'Ufficio federale di statistica (UST) è il centro di competenza nazionale della statistica pubblica e il fulcro dell'ecosistema dei dati svizzero. Produce informazioni statistiche, si impegna, attraverso la gestione dei dati a livello nazionale, a favore di dati armonizzati e utilizza metodi di scienza dei dati per estrarre ulteriori informazioni dai dati. In questo modo l'UST contribuisce a formare l'opinione pubblica fornendo un importante contributo alla moderna governance democratica.
