Title: Coordinator Business Support (50%)
Description:
Coordinator Business Support (50%)

 

Your Mission
In this role, you will be part of the McDonald’s Switzerland Business Support Team, a key team supporting multiple departments and managers on a daily basis. In this role, you will contribute to the smooth running of administrative and organizational activities by providing reliable, proactive, and high-quality support. You will work closely with other assistants and act in a transversal role depending on business needs.  

 

Key Responsibilities

Provide administrative support to the Marketing department as well as other teams as needed
Manage calendars, organize meetings, visits, and events, including all related logistics
Maintain and update internal tools and databases, ensuring data quality and confidentiality
Follow up on various administrative topics and contribute to process improvements
Ensure administrative follow-up of Marketing budgets in collaboration with the fiduciary
Collaborate closely with other assistants in the pool and with different teams
Anticipate needs and contribute to smooth coordination between stakeholders

 

What You Need to Be Successful

Proven experience in a similar role within a demanding environment (large company)
Strong organizational skills, high level of accuracy, confidentiality, and ability to work under
pressure
Native French and/or German, with knowledge of the other language considered an asset,
and fluent in English (minimum B2)
Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Office 365, AI, etc.)
Discretion, proactivity, autonomy, service-oriented mindset, and analytical skills
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced, dynamic, and changing environment
Strong interest in organizing and coordinating events
Availability to work on a part-time schedule: Wednesday mandatory, and either
Monday/Tuesday and either Thursday/Friday

 

With us, you get the following benefits

Flexible working: the ability to work remotely to achieve the best possible balance between
work and private life. Home Office is possible. Working hours are flexible in agreement with
the leader, taking into account business and individual needs.
Bonus: a variable percentage based on performance and commercial results.
Active talent management: targeted engagement in personal learning and skills
development, on-site, via e-learning, or on the lively campus of Hamburger University in
London, Paris, and Munich.
Employee well-being: contributions to wellness activities.
Attractive discounts in our restaurants and food for employees.
Program packed with exclusive discounts on our partner platform, corporate benefits.

Title: Intern Data Science & Machine Learning (80-100%)
Description:
Electricity flows with us. On behalf of Switzerland and in cooperation with international partners, we do our utmost every day to develop the best solutions for the Swiss electricity grid.

Our team in Aarau is looking for an

Intern Data Science & Machine Learning (80-100%) - 1583

Power Switzerland's Energy Future – From Day One

At Swissgrid, we don't just observe the energy transition, we engineer it. As the operator of Switzerland's national transmission grid, we sit at the nexus of a rapidly evolving European electricity market. Our Market Optimization & Decision Support team develops forecasting and automation solutions that directly support grid operators in their 24/7 mission to ensure a reliable power supply. By joining us, you will contribute to impactful work at the heart of Switzerland’s electricity system.

The Internship: Where Learning Meets Real Impact

You’ll work on challenging and meaningful data science and machine learning projects. You’ll gain unique insights into the Swiss transmission system while working alongside a motivated, international team of data scientists and engineers. The work is real from day one—real data, real decisions, and real impact. You’ll have access to a modern, cloud-native tech stack and the latest AI development tools, and you’ll be encouraged to bring and run with your creative, AI-driven approaches to complex data problems.

What You Will Work On

Roll up your sleeves for challenges like these:


Build and ship forecasting models that solve real energy-sector problems with a sharp focus on safety, scalability, and production robustness. You'll experiment freely: tuning hyperparameters, benchmarking architectures, and proposing novel approaches.
Design and build data pipelines that process large-scale time-series data reliably and efficiently, forming the backbone of model training and deployment workflows.
Explore and analyze data surface hidden patterns, engineer high-signal features, and push forecasting accuracy further.
Work AI-natively – integrate AI tools into your daily development practice to sharpen analysis, accelerate iteration, smoothen workflows and improve efficiencies.
Collaborate and communicate across teams, gather feedback from stakeholders, and make sure your work connects meaningfully to the broader business mission.


What Makes You a Great Fit

We are looking for a builder – someone with the curiosity to experiment and the discipline to deliver. You'll thrive here if you bring:


Academic foundation: Currently pursuing (or recently completed) a Bachelor's or Master's in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Economics, or a related field.
Strong Python skills and solid software engineering instincts – version control, clean code, and reproducible workflows are second nature to you.
Machine learning depth: A genuine understanding of classical ML, statistical methods, time-series modeling, and predictive systems. Hands-on experience with libraries like Scikit-learn, PyTorch, or MLflow is a plus; familiarity with Apache Spark is a bonus.
AI-native mindset: You use GenAI tools to build things, not just to ask questions. Show us what you've already shipped.
Energy domain curiosity: Knowledge of transmission grid operations or electricity markets is a plus – but your drive to learn matters more.
Above all, we value curiosity, agency, and a growth mindset. If you're the kind of person who gets energized by hard problems and wants your work to matter, we'd love to hear from you. 🚀


What We Offer


Unique industry access – an immersive, behind-the-scenes look at Switzerland's electricity infrastructure and the challenges of building the grid of the future.
Meaningful work – varied and exciting tasks within a company unique to Switzerland., with real ownership from the start.
Flexibility – flexible working hours as part of a motivated, welcoming team.
25 days of holiday, plus company holidays between Christmas and New Year.
A modern workplace in the immediate vicinity of Aarau, with a state-of-the-art technical environment.

Title: Business Intelligence Engineer
Description:
Your Role

As Business Intelligence Engineer at Bank J. Safra Sarasin you are working closely with Business Departments, analyzing their requirements and determining data reporting needs that support business decision making. You will be responsible for the delivery of reports and dashboards and provide insights through BI visualization technologies. You will be part of a dynamic team of highly qualified employees and will be encouraged to contribute your own ideas, continue learning, develop concepts, and put them into practice.

Your Responsibilities


Develop PowerBI dashboards
Recommend enhancements and modifications to optimize business intelligence processes
Develop product vision and data strategy
Monitor and troubleshoot of daily data processing
Support of strategic projects as BI specialist
Maintain documentations for all business intelligence processes


Your Profile


Higher education (university / FH) in IT Engineering, Computer Sciences, or Data and Analytics
5 years of practical experience in Business Intelligence; industry specific knowledge is an advantage
Knowledge in Microsoft BI Development Suite (SQL Server (T-SQL), SSIS, SSAS, SSRS, Power BI) is required
A hands-on, autonomous professional with strong analytical and problem-solving skills, capable of effectively weighing issues, setting priorities, and collaborating with various stakeholders
Fluency in English


Your Benefits


Entrepreneurial spirit in a family owned bank
Central city office locations across Switzerland
Above average insurance coverage fully borne by the bank
Contribution to health insurance and meal allowance
Global growth strategy and stable environment
Competitive offering

Title: Senior Data Engineer
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Senior Data Engineer based in Switzerland.

As a Senior Data Engineer, you will own the complete data lifecycle, from ingestion and infrastructure to modeling, analytics, and business insights. Working in a fully remote, high-growth environment, you will design scalable data platforms that support strategic decision-making across product, finance, and growth teams. This role offers the opportunity to build resilient, production-grade data systems while leveraging modern cloud technologies and AI-powered development tools. You will collaborate closely with cross-functional stakeholders, solve complex data challenges at scale, and play a critical role in shaping a data-driven organization. If you enjoy end-to-end ownership, continuous innovation, and building systems that can grow alongside the business, this is an excellent opportunity to make a lasting impact.

Accountabilities


Design, build, and maintain scalable data pipelines from source systems through data warehouses and analytics platforms.
Develop and optimize data ingestion workflows using cloud-native technologies, orchestration tools, and Python-based data loaders.
Create and maintain reliable data models that enable accurate reporting, business intelligence, and self-service analytics.
Deliver actionable analyses that explain business performance and support strategic decision-making.
Build data infrastructure with scalability, reliability, performance, and cost optimization as core design principles.
Manage infrastructure as code to ensure consistent, automated deployment and maintenance of the data platform.
Partner closely with product, finance, growth, and engineering teams to translate business questions into trusted data solutions.
Leverage AI-powered development tools to improve engineering productivity while maintaining full ownership of production quality.
Monitor, troubleshoot, and continuously improve data pipelines and platform performance.
Stay current with emerging technologies and best practices in data engineering, cloud infrastructure, and artificial intelligence.


Requirements


5+ years of experience designing, building, and operating production-grade data pipelines and modern data platforms.
Strong expertise with cloud data technologies such as Google Cloud Platform, BigQuery, dbt, Airflow, Dataflow, or equivalent solutions.
Experience with infrastructure as code using tools such as Pulumi or Terraform.
Advanced SQL skills and proficiency in Python for data engineering and automation.
Experience working with analytics platforms and transforming complex datasets into business insights.
Familiarity with AI-assisted development tools and an understanding of how to apply them responsibly within engineering workflows.
Strong analytical thinking with the ability to identify root causes behind business metrics and performance changes.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively across technical and non-technical teams.
Comfortable working independently in a fast-paced, remote-first environment with a high level of ownership.
Passion for scalable data engineering, continuous learning, and building reliable systems that support business growth.


Benefits


Fully remote position with the flexibility to work from anywhere.
Co-working space membership or allowance to support your preferred work environment.
Equity participation through an employee stock ownership program (ESOP).
Technology allowance to build your ideal home office setup.
Comprehensive health insurance, including full employee coverage and substantial dependent coverage.
Flexible, asynchronous work schedule that promotes autonomy and work-life balance.
Annual professional development budget for courses, conferences, books, and learning resources.
Annual company off-site events that bring together global team members for collaboration and networking.
Opportunity to work with modern cloud technologies, large-scale data systems, and cutting-edge AI tools.
Collaborative, international environment focused on innovation, ownership, and continuous growth.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Description:
CLEEVEN est un cabinet de conseil européen fondé autour de deux convictions fondamentales :

L'excellence des ingénieurs européens doit être un moteur de la compétitivité mondiale
Notre mission humaine : accompagner nos consultants dans leur développement personnel et professionnel

Présent en Suisse, nous accompagnons nos clients dans leurs projets de transformation digitale et de valorisation de la donnée.




Contexte du projet :

Nous recherchons un Data Analyst / BI Engineer pour accompagner le développement et l'évolution de solutions de reporting et d'analyse décisionnelle.

Vous interviendrez au croisement des enjeux métiers et techniques afin de transformer des données complexes en indicateurs de pilotage et en outils d'aide à la décision. Vous participerez à l'ensemble de la chaîne de valeur de la donnée : compréhension des besoins, modélisation, construction de tableaux de bord et amélioration continue des solutions analytiques.




Objectifs du projet :

Recueillir et analyser les besoins de reporting et d'analyse des équipes métiers.
Concevoir, développer et maintenir des tableaux de bord et solutions de reporting.
Extraire, transformer et analyser des données provenant de multiples sources.
Construire et faire évoluer des modèles de données et des couches sémantiques.
Définir et suivre des KPIs permettant de piloter l'activité.
Garantir la qualité, la cohérence et la fiabilité des données.
Accompagner les utilisateurs dans l'exploitation des outils décisionnels.
Participer à l'amélioration continue de l'écosystème BI et Data de l'entreprise.




Compétences clés :

Solides compétences en SQL et en bases de données relationnelles.
Expérience sur des outils de Business Intelligence et de visualisation de données.
Bonne maîtrise des concepts de modélisation de données.
Capacité à traduire des besoins métiers en solutions de reporting concrètes.
Excellentes capacités d'analyse et de résolution de problèmes.
Aisance dans les échanges avec des interlocuteurs métiers et techniques.
Bon niveau d'anglais professionnel.

Nice To Have

Expérience avec Looker et/ou LookML.
Expérience avec Power BI, Tableau ou des outils similaires.
Connaissances des concepts ETL/ELT et des pipelines de données.
Expérience sur des plateformes cloud (GCP, Azure ou AWS).
Expérience dans un environnement industriel ou manufacturier.
Connaissances de Python pour l'analyse de données ou l'automatisation.




Profil recherché :

Formation supérieure en informatique, data, systèmes d'information, mathématiques, statistiques ou ingénierie.
Expérience en tant que Data Analyst, BI Engineer ou sur un poste similaire.
Forte capacité d'analyse et sens du détail.
Capacité à comprendre les enjeux métiers et à transformer les données en informations exploitables.




À très vite !

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Develop & Scale Pipelines: You design and implement robust ETL/ELT pipelines to process large, complex volumes of data (Big Data) using both code-based and visual tools.
Semantic Data Modeling: You translate complex business processes into logical data models and help build a centralized, cross-departmental enterprise data model.
Data Quality & Lineage: You implement automated quality checks and monitor data flows from source to end product to guarantee seamless data lineage.
Code & Transformation: You write clean, high-performance code in PySpark and SQL to clean, aggregate, and prepare data for downstream analysis.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: You work closely with Data Scientists, Business Analysts, and business departments to quickly translate their requirements into production-ready data structures.
Initial practical experience in Data Engineering (as a Junior, e.g., through relevant internships/working student roles; as a Mid-Level, approx. 1–3 years of professional experience).
Solid knowledge of Python (especially in a Big Data context with PySpark) as well as proficiency in writing complex SQL queries.
Good understanding of modern data architectures (Data Lakehouse, distributed systems) and ideally experience with integrated all-in-one data platforms.
Familiar with relational and non-relational databases. You enjoy bringing order to unstructured mountains of data.
Analytical mindset, work in a structured manner, and possess a strong willingness to learn, allowing you to quickly adapt to new, complex software ecosystems.
Start date : September
Location : Zurich - CH
Duration : permanent contract
Language : fluent in English, German is a plus


Who are we?

The SII Group is at the heart of innovation, serving major accounts in a variety of engineering sectors. In 2026, for the ninth consecutive year, SII France was awarded the Great Place To Work® label.

This success reflects our culture, which is based on our desire to offer all our employees a fulfilling working environment in which they can develop their skills and careers.

Joining SII means embarking on a collective adventure: Let's Tech Together!

This promise represents our shared ambition: to build a technological future together, in an environment where experimentation and autonomy are encouraged, where innovation is at the heart of everything we do, and where every employee finds professional fulfilment and empowerment within an enthusiastic and inclusive team.

As a company with strong human values, we are signatories to the LGBT+ Commitment Charter with the Association l'autre Cercle and are a disability-friendly company.

Let's Tech Together embodies who we are and what we aspire to be: a dynamic, innovative and people-oriented company. So if these values resonate with you, join us!

Title: Customer Success Manager
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Customer Success Manager based in Switzerland.

As a Customer Success Manager, you will own the success and growth of a portfolio of strategic customers by helping them achieve measurable business outcomes. You will work at the intersection of customers, product, sales, and engineering teams to improve adoption, efficiency, and long-term value. This role goes beyond traditional account management, combining customer advocacy, technical understanding, and commercial expertise to drive meaningful impact. You will lead improvement initiatives, identify growth opportunities, and use data-driven insights to strengthen customer relationships. In a fast-moving, remote-first environment, you will have the autonomy to solve complex problems and contribute to the evolution of innovative AI-powered solutions.

Accountabilities


Own a portfolio of Growth and Strategic customer accounts from post-onboarding through long-term success, ensuring measurable outcomes and customer value.
Develop and execute customer success plans aligned with operational goals, customer experience metrics, and business objectives.
Analyze customer performance, identify improvement opportunities, and lead structured action plans with clear ownership and timelines.
Drive improvements across key success metrics, including automation rates, resolution efficiency, customer satisfaction, retention, and expansion.
Manage renewal conversations and proactively identify, develop, and close expansion opportunities based on demonstrated customer value.
Build strong relationships with senior stakeholders, including customer experience leaders and executives, while confidently navigating business discussions.
Partner with product, engineering, sales, and services teams to translate customer needs into actionable solutions and product improvements.
Provide technical guidance, explain product capabilities, support integrations, and recommend best practices for complex use cases.
Lead customer education initiatives, including training sessions, workshops, webinars, documentation, and product enablement materials.
Contribute customer insights, competitive intelligence, and feedback to internal teams to improve product strategy.
Mentor colleagues and support team development through knowledge sharing and collaboration.


Requirements


3+ years of experience in Customer Success Management, account management, or strategic client-facing roles involving technical and complex software solutions.
Previous experience working in startup environments, with the ability to adapt quickly, manage ambiguity, and take ownership of outcomes.
Proven success managing enterprise customers, including renewals, expansions, and commercial negotiations; experience closing a $100k+ deal is preferred.
Strong experience working with complex organizations and multiple stakeholders across customer accounts.
Background in e-commerce, customer experience, SaaS, AI, automation, or related technology industries is strongly preferred.
Experience collaborating closely with product and engineering teams to deliver customer outcomes.
Technical understanding of AI/ML solutions, conversational AI, automation platforms, APIs, and workflow design.
Hands-on experience with prompt engineering, AI agent development, conversation optimization, or building AI-powered workflows.
Ability to analyze customer usage data, market trends, and business metrics to create actionable strategies.
Strong commercial mindset with the ability to connect customer outcomes to cost savings, efficiency improvements, and revenue impact.
Excellent communication, presentation, problem-solving, and stakeholder management skills.
Self-directed, proactive, curious, and comfortable challenging existing processes to achieve better results.
Ability to operate effectively in a fast-paced, remote, and continuously evolving environment.


Benefits


Competitive salary package with equity or stock grant opportunities.
Fully remote work environment with flexibility to work from anywhere.
High level of ownership and autonomy with the opportunity to solve meaningful customer and business challenges.
Opportunity to work on cutting-edge AI technology and contribute to building a new category of solutions.
Learning and development budget to support professional growth.
Access to premium AI tools and resources to enhance productivity and experimentation.
Globally distributed, async-first culture with low bureaucracy and strong focus on trust and ownership.
Opportunity to work closely with innovative brands and deliver solutions that create measurable business impact.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Internship - Analytics Engineer - Studentjob.ch
Description:
Company Description

Sika is a specialty chemicals company with a globally leading position in the development and production of systems and products for bonding, sealing, damping, reinforcing, and protection in the building sector and industry. Sika has subsidiaries in 103 countries around the world, produces in over 400 factories, and develops innovative technologies for customers worldwide. In doing so, it plays a crucial role in enabling the transformation of the construction and transportation industries toward greater environmental compatibility. Approximately 33,000 employees generated CHF 11.20 billion in sales in 2025.

Job Description



Design, implementation, and maintenance of ETL processes to ensure a structured and reliable data foundation
Automation and optimization of data processes and recurring workflows (e.g. Using Python and SQL)
Close collaboration with business stakeholders to translate business requirements into data-driven solutions
Support for Finance and Controlling in data analysis, reporting, and performance tracking
Development and continuous improvement of BI dashboards as well as scalable reporting solutions
Identification and implementation of optimization potentials in existing processes and systems
Use of modern technologies (including AI tools) to increase efficiency and further develop analyses and processes



Qualifications



Completed Bachelor's or Master's degree in Data Science, Business Analytics, Computer Science, or a related field
Strong knowledge of data analysis, data modeling, and data management
Hands-on experience with SQL and Python for data processing and automation
Experience with BI tools (e.g. Power BI)
Solid knowledge of developing and using ETL processes and data pipelines

Title: Head of Data & AI
Description:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Head of Data & AI based in Switzerland.

As the Head of Data & AI, you will lead the strategic vision and execution of a unified data organization spanning Data Engineering, Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, Quantitative Analytics, and Business Intelligence. In this leadership role, you will shape scalable data platforms, drive AI innovation, and build high-performing teams that transform complex data into measurable business value. Working at the intersection of engineering, analytics, and emerging AI technologies, you'll define technical roadmaps, establish best practices, and collaborate with senior stakeholders to support business growth. This is an outstanding opportunity for an experienced data leader to influence both technology strategy and organizational direction in a fast-paced, innovation-driven environment.

Accountabilities


Define and execute the overall Data & AI strategy, aligning data engineering, analytics, quantitative research, and AI initiatives with long-term business objectives.
Lead and mentor multidisciplinary teams while establishing technical standards, career development frameworks, and a culture of collaboration and continuous improvement.
Design and evolve scalable, reliable, and secure data platform architectures, overseeing ETL/ELT pipelines, real-time data processing, and cloud infrastructure.
Drive the development and implementation of AI and Large Language Model (LLM) solutions, including retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), AI agents, intelligent automation, and semantic search capabilities.
Establish best practices for Data Engineering, MLOps, and LLMOps, including CI/CD, model versioning, monitoring, observability, prompt management, and performance evaluation.
Ensure high standards for data quality, governance, security, compliance, and responsible AI practices, including fairness, explainability, and regulatory alignment.
Partner with business stakeholders to translate strategic challenges into data-driven solutions, delivering executive dashboards, advanced analytics, experimentation frameworks, and measurable business outcomes.
Optimize technology selection, infrastructure performance, and cloud resource utilization while managing platform scalability and operational efficiency.


Requirements


Minimum of 7 years of hands-on experience across Data Engineering, Data Science, Machine Learning, Quantitative Analytics, and Data Analytics, delivering end-to-end data solutions.
At least 3 years of leadership experience managing technical data teams and driving organizational growth.
Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Physics, or a related quantitative discipline.
Strong programming expertise in Python and experience building production-grade data and machine learning applications.
Deep understanding of software engineering best practices, including CI/CD, testing, clean architecture, code reviews, and DevOps principles.
Extensive experience with machine learning algorithms, statistical modeling, quantitative analysis, and frameworks such as scikit-learn, XGBoost, TensorFlow, or PyTorch.
Proven expertise with SQL, analytical databases, OLAP technologies, and modern data platform architectures.
Strong experience defining AI strategies, implementing LLM-powered applications, and working with vector databases, embedding models, and AI governance frameworks.
Excellent communication, stakeholder management, and leadership skills with the ability to align technical initiatives with business priorities.
Professional proficiency in English. Experience in fintech, trading, fraud detection, risk modeling, portfolio analytics, or enterprise LLM integration is considered an advantage.


Benefits


Fully remote working opportunity with flexible work arrangements.
Competitive compensation package.
20 days of paid annual leave.
10 days of paid sick leave.
Public holidays in accordance with local legislation.
Comprehensive medical insurance.
Annual professional development and education budget.
Language learning allowance.
Wellness budget covering gym memberships, sports equipment, and related health expenses.
Opportunity to shape the strategy and future of a growing Data & AI organization while working with modern technologies and advanced AI solutions.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. Title: MTS - Data Analysis and Automated Reports Development
Description:
Are you ready to shape the future of capital markets? We are looking for a Data Analysis and Automated Reports Development specialist to join the MTS team. The position will be based in Rome, and start as soon as possible. You will report to the Market Surveillance Manager.

Starting annual gross salary - 35.600 euro

You Will Primarily Be Responsible For


Writing technical documentation for internal and external use
Maintaining web front-end pages for clients and internal management
Checking and analysing market data and generating periodic reports for management, sales and customers
Collaborating with development teams and product managers to implement software solutions
Developing new reports for market participants, issuers, universities and internal management
Ensuring data accuracy, integrity and security in all reporting activities


Your Profile


2 to 3 years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence or IT roles, ideally within financial markets or market infrastructure
Degree in engineering, computer science or a related field
Strong knowledge of Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Access, Power BI)
Basic knowledge of Microsoft Office VBA and .NET programming (Visual Studio)
Familiarity with Windows Server OS, Windows 11 client OS and SQL/SQL Server
Understanding of web server OS, JavaScript, PHP and FTP/SFTP server management
Excellent analytical mindset, attention to detail and strong problem-solving skills
Ability to write clear technical documentation and communicate effectively with both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Team-oriented, proactive and able to manage multiple tasks simultaneously
Fluent in Italian and English


Join a Company Where Market Infrastructure Is Built On Innovation - And Where Every Innovation Starts With a Bold Mind Asking, “What’s Next?”. At Euronext, We Believe Change Is a Starting Point, Not An Obstacle. We Look For People Who See Growth Opportunities, Think Long Term And Want To Help Shape Capital Markets For Future Generations. Working With Us Means


Being part of a European, international and diverse team – Collaborate across borders with talented colleagues connected by a shared ambition and purpose.
Learning as you grow – Develop your skills through continuous learning, real responsibilities and flexible career paths that evolve with you.
Having an impact – Contribute to projects that matter, in an agile and dynamic environment where your ideas help drive progress.
Driving sustainability – Play an active role in building transparent, responsible and sustainable capital markets for the long term. Together, we do not just support markets - we help shape their future.


Apply by submitting a CV in English.

MTS (part of the Euronext Group) is a leading operator of regulated electronic platforms and market data services for the European fixed income market, supporting daily transaction volumes of more than €265 billion (2025). With a rich history spanning over three decades, MTS has established itself as a cornerstone of the fixed income trading community, known for its innovation, reliability and comprehensive service offerings.

Join Euronext – Shape capital markets for future generations Euronext is the leading European capital market covering the entire capital markets value chain, from listing, trading, clearing, settlement and custody, to solutions for issuers and investors. We operate seven national stock exchanges, handling 25% of European lit equity trading. Our products include equities, FX, ETFs, bonds, derivatives, commodities and indices. Euronext also provides clearing and settlement services through Euronext Clearing and our network of Euronext Securities CSDs. In addition, Euronext runs MTS, one of Europe’s leading electronic fixed income trading markets, and Nord Pool, the European power market. The company has a diverse domestic and international client base.

Find out more about us at: Euronext.com

We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate against individuals on the basis of race, gender, age, citizenship, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability, or any other legally protected factor. We value the unique talents of all our people, who come from diverse backgrounds with different personal experiences and points of view and we are committed to providing an environment of mutual respect.

Additional Information

This job description is only describing the main activities within a certain role and is not exhaustive. It does not prevent to add more tasks, projects.
